task: In which year-month were the most musical instrument reviews posted? Report as YYYY-MM (e.g. '2020-01').

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH mi AS (
  SELECT ref_id FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL SELECT ref_id FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL SELECT ref_id FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments'
),
r AS (
  SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', ts/1000, 'unixepoch') AS ym FROM fdbk_g1 WHERE ref_id IN (SELECT ref_id FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments')
  UNION ALL
  SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', ts, 'unixepoch') FROM fdbk_g2 WHERE ref_id IN (SELECT ref_id FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments')
  UNION ALL
  SELECT substr(ts,1,7) FROM fdbk_g3 WHERE ref_id IN (SELECT ref_id FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments')
)
SELECT ym, COUNT(*) c FROM r GROUP BY ym ORDER BY c DESC LIMIT 10;"

task: For musical instruments, how much higher (in star rating) is the average review rating for products with a listed price compared to products without a price? Round to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH combined AS (
  SELECT prc, avg_rtg FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT prc, avg_rtg FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT prc, avg_rtg FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments'
)
SELECT
  AVG(CASE WHEN prc IS NOT NULL THEN avg_rtg END) AS with_price,
  AVG(CASE WHEN prc IS NULL THEN avg_rtg END) AS no_price,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN prc IS NOT NULL THEN 1 END) AS n_with,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN prc IS NULL THEN 1 END) AS n_without
FROM combined WHERE avg_rtg IS NOT NULL;"

task: How many office products have reviews spanning 5 or more distinct calendar years? Exclude reviews with corrupted timestamps (dates in 2031 or later).

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH ap AS (
  SELECT ref_id FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='Office Products'
  UNION ALL SELECT ref_id FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='Office Products'
  UNION ALL SELECT ref_id FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='Office Products'
),
yrs AS (
  SELECT f.ref_id, CAST(strftime('%Y', f.ts/1000, 'unixepoch') AS INT) AS y FROM fdbk_g1 f WHERE f.ref_id IN (SELECT ref_id FROM ap)
  UNION ALL
  SELECT f.ref_id, CAST(strftime('%Y', f.ts, 'unixepoch') AS INT) AS y FROM fdbk_g2 f WHERE f.ref_id IN (SELECT ref_id FROM ap)
  UNION ALL
  SELECT f.ref_id, CAST(substr(f.ts,1,4) AS INT) AS y FROM fdbk_g3 f WHERE f.ref_id IN (SELECT ref_id FROM ap) AND f.ts IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT ref_id FROM yrs WHERE y<2031 GROUP BY ref_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT y)>=5);
"

task: What fraction of musical instrument reviews from repeat purchasers (users who have left 3 or more reviews in this category) are 5-star? Express as a percentage rounded to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH mi_reviews AS (
  SELECT f.uid, f.rtg FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT f.uid, f.rtg FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT f.uid, f.rtg FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'
),
repeat_users AS (
  SELECT uid FROM mi_reviews WHERE uid IS NOT NULL GROUP BY uid HAVING COUNT(*)>=3
)
SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN m.rtg=5.0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS five, COUNT(*) AS total, ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN m.rtg=5.0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/COUNT(*),2)
FROM mi_reviews m JOIN repeat_users r ON m.uid=r.uid;
"

task: Among electronics reviews with a review body longer than 500 characters, considering only products whose list price is at most $40, what percentage are 1-star? Round to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH revs AS (
  SELECT f.rtg FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
    WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND i.prc IS NOT NULL AND i.prc<=40 AND LENGTH(f.body)>500
  UNION ALL
  SELECT f.rtg FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
    WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND i.prc_usd IS NOT NULL AND i.prc_usd<=40 AND LENGTH(f.body)>500
  UNION ALL
  SELECT f.rtg FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
    WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND i.prc IS NOT NULL AND i.prc<=40 AND LENGTH(f.body)>500
)
SELECT COUNT(*) total, SUM(CASE WHEN rtg=1.0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) onestar,
  ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN rtg=1.0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/COUNT(*),2) pct
FROM revs;"

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH ca AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT i.ref_id, i.prc_usd AS price
  FROM items_g2 i
  JOIN taxn_g2 t ON t.ref_id = i.ref_id
  WHERE t.cat_lvl=1 AND t.cat_nm='Computers & Accessories'
    AND i.prc_usd IS NOT NULL AND i.prc_usd != '' AND i.prc_usd > 0
),
avg AS (SELECT AVG(price) a FROM ca)
SELECT COUNT(*), SUM(CASE WHEN price > (SELECT a FROM avg) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END),
ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN price > (SELECT a FROM avg) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/COUNT(*),2)
FROM ca;"

task: For verified electronics reviews, how much longer on average (in characters) are 1-star review bodies compared to 5-star review bodies? Round to 1 decimal place. Only count reviews with non-empty body text.

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH allr AS (
  SELECT f.rtg AS rtg, length(f.body) AS L
  FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND f.vrf=1 AND f.body IS NOT NULL AND trim(f.body)<>''
  UNION ALL
  SELECT f.rtg, length(f.body)
  FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND f.vrf='true' AND f.body IS NOT NULL AND trim(f.body)<>''
  UNION ALL
  SELECT f.rtg, length(f.body)
  FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND f.vrf=1 AND f.body IS NOT NULL AND trim(f.body)<>''
)
SELECT rtg, count(*), avg(L) FROM allr WHERE rtg IN (1.0,5.0) GROUP BY rtg;
"

task: Among musical instrument brands with at least 20 products (using the attributes data), which brand has the highest average review rating? Report the brand name.

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
SELECT a.attr_val AS brand, COUNT(DISTINCT i.ref_id) AS n, AVG(i.avg_rtg) AS avg_item_rtg
FROM items_g3 i
JOIN attrs_g3 a ON a.ref_id = i.ref_id AND a.attr_key='Brand'
WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'
GROUP BY a.attr_val
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT i.ref_id) >= 20
ORDER BY avg_item_rtg DESC
LIMIT 15;"

task: Among musical instrument brands with at least 20 products (using the attributes data), which brand has the highest average review rating? Report the brand name.

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
SELECT a.attr_val AS brand, COUNT(DISTINCT i.ref_id) AS n_products, AVG(f.rtg) AS avg_review_rtg
FROM items_g3 i
JOIN attrs_g3 a ON a.ref_id = i.ref_id AND a.attr_key='Brand'
JOIN fdbk_g3 f ON f.ref_id = i.ref_id
WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'
GROUP BY a.attr_val
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT i.ref_id) >= 20
ORDER BY avg_review_rtg DESC
LIMIT 10;"

task: For how many office products does the stored average rating differ from the actual mean of its review scores by more than 1 full star?

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH prod AS (
  SELECT ref_id, avg_rtg FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='Office Products'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT ref_id, avg_rtg FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='Office Products'
),
fb AS (
  SELECT ref_id, rtg FROM fdbk_g1
  UNION ALL
  SELECT ref_id, rtg FROM fdbk_g2
),
means AS (
  SELECT p.ref_id, p.avg_rtg, AVG(fb.rtg) AS actual
  FROM prod p JOIN fb ON fb.ref_id=p.ref_id
  GROUP BY p.ref_id, p.avg_rtg
)
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM means WHERE ABS(avg_rtg - actual) > 1;
"

task: How many office products have at least one review in the database but are NOT represented in the attributes data at all?

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH office_items AS (
  SELECT ref_id, 'g1' g FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='Office Products'
  UNION ALL SELECT ref_id, 'g2' FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='Office Products'
  UNION ALL SELECT ref_id, 'g3' FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='Office Products'
),
reviewed AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT ref_id FROM fdbk_g1
  UNION SELECT DISTINCT ref_id FROM fdbk_g2
  UNION SELECT DISTINCT ref_id FROM fdbk_g3
),
attributed AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT ref_id FROM attrs_g1
  UNION SELECT DISTINCT ref_id FROM attrs_g3
)
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM office_items o
WHERE o.ref_id IN reviewed AND o.ref_id NOT IN attributed;
"

task: How many office products have at least one review in the database but are NOT represented in the attributes data at all?

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH office_items AS (
  SELECT ref_id, 'g1' g FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='Office Products'
  UNION ALL SELECT ref_id, 'g2' FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='Office Products'
  UNION ALL SELECT ref_id, 'g3' FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='Office Products'
)
SELECT g, COUNT(*), 
  SUM(CASE WHEN ref_id IN (SELECT ref_id FROM fdbk_g1 UNION SELECT ref_id FROM fdbk_g2 UNION SELECT ref_id FROM fdbk_g3) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) reviewed,
  SUM(CASE WHEN ref_id NOT IN (SELECT ref_id FROM attrs_g1 UNION SELECT ref_id FROM attrs_g3) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) no_attrs
FROM office_items GROUP BY g;
"

task: How many electronics products priced above $50 have at least 5 reviews and an average review rating below 3.0?

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (
  SELECT i.ref_id FROM items_g1 i
  JOIN fdbk_g1 f ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND i.prc>50
  GROUP BY i.ref_id
  HAVING COUNT(f.id)>=5 AND AVG(f.rtg)<3.0
);
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (
  SELECT i.ref_id FROM items_g2 i
  JOIN fdbk_g2 f ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND i.prc_usd>50
  GROUP BY i.ref_id
  HAVING COUNT(f.id)>=5 AND AVG(f.rtg)<3.0
);
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (
  SELECT i.ref_id FROM items_g3 i
  JOIN fdbk_g3 f ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND i.prc>50
  GROUP BY i.ref_id
  HAVING COUNT(f.id)>=5 AND AVG(f.rtg)<3.0
);"

task: In which year-month were the most musical instrument reviews posted? Report as YYYY-MM (e.g. '2020-01').

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH combined AS (
  SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', datetime(f.ts/1000, 'unixepoch')) AS ym
  FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', datetime(f.ts, 'unixepoch')) AS ym
  FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT substr(f.ts,1,7) AS ym
  FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'
)
SELECT ym, COUNT(*) c FROM combined WHERE ym IS NOT NULL GROUP BY ym ORDER BY c DESC LIMIT 10;
"

  (SELECT AVG(avg_rtg) FROM (
    SELECT avg_rtg FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments' AND prc IS NOT NULL AND avg_rtg IS NOT NULL
    UNION ALL SELECT avg_rtg FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments' AND prc IS NOT NULL AND avg_rtg IS NOT NULL

  (SELECT AVG(avg_rtg) FROM (
    SELECT avg_rtg FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments' AND prc IS NULL AND avg_rtg IS NOT NULL
    UNION ALL SELECT avg_rtg FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments' AND prc IS NULL AND avg_rtg IS NOT NULL
    UNION ALL SELECT avg_rtg FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments' AND prc IS NULL AND avg_rtg IS NOT NULL

task: Across both product categories that store attribute data, how many total products are listed as discontinued by the manufacturer and have a catalog average star rating strictly below 3.0?

arguments.command: sqlite3 database.db "SELECT attr_val, COUNT(*) FROM attrs_g1 WHERE attr_key IN ('Is Discontinued By Manufacturer','Is Discontinued by Manufacturer') GROUP BY attr_val; SELECT '---'; SELECT attr_val, COUNT(*) FROM attrs_g3 WHERE attr_key IN ('Is Discontinued By Manufacturer','Is Discontinued by Manufacturer') GROUP BY attr_val;"

observation.content:
False|4
No|7974
Yes|144
false|2
---
False|5
No|9388
True|1
Yes|91
false|5

task: Across both product categories that store attribute data, how many total products are listed as discontinued by the manufacturer and have a catalog average star rating strictly below 3.0?

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT i.ref_id) FROM items_g1 i JOIN attrs_g1 a ON i.ref_id=a.ref_id
WHERE a.attr_key IN ('Is Discontinued By Manufacturer','Is Discontinued by Manufacturer')
AND a.attr_val IN ('Yes','True','true') AND i.avg_rtg < 3.0;
SELECT '---';
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT i.ref_id) FROM items_g3 i JOIN attrs_g3 a ON i.ref_id=a.ref_id
WHERE a.attr_key IN ('Is Discontinued By Manufacturer','Is Discontinued by Manufacturer')
AND a.attr_val IN ('Yes','True','true') AND i.avg_rtg < 3.0;"

task: How many office products have reviews spanning 5 or more distinct calendar years? Exclude reviews with corrupted timestamps (dates in 2031 or later).

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH years AS (
  SELECT i.ref_id, CAST(strftime('%Y', f.ts/1000, 'unixepoch') AS INT) AS y
  FROM items_g1 i JOIN fdbk_g1 f ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products' AND f.ts IS NOT NULL
  UNION
  SELECT i.ref_id, CAST(strftime('%Y', f.ts, 'unixepoch') AS INT) AS y
  FROM items_g2 i JOIN fdbk_g2 f ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products' AND f.ts IS NOT NULL
  UNION
  SELECT i.ref_id, CAST(substr(f.ts,1,4) AS INT) AS y
  FROM items_g3 i JOIN fdbk_g3 f ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products' AND f.ts IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (
  SELECT ref_id FROM years WHERE y < 2031 GROUP BY ref_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT y) >= 5
);
"

task: What fraction of musical instrument reviews from repeat purchasers (users who have left 3 or more reviews in this category) are 5-star? Express as a percentage rounded to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "WITH mi_reviews AS (
  SELECT f.uid, f.rtg FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT f.uid, f.rtg FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT f.uid, f.rtg FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'
),
repeat_users AS (
  SELECT uid FROM mi_reviews GROUP BY uid HAVING COUNT(*)>=3
)
SELECT ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN rtg=5 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/COUNT(*),2), COUNT(*), SUM(CASE WHEN rtg=5 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
FROM mi_reviews WHERE uid IN (SELECT uid FROM repeat_users);"

task: What fraction of musical instrument reviews from repeat purchasers (users who have left 3 or more reviews in this category) are 5-star? Express as a percentage rounded to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "WITH mi_reviews AS (
  SELECT f.uid, f.rtg FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'
),
repeat_users AS (
  SELECT uid FROM mi_reviews GROUP BY uid HAVING COUNT(*)>=3
)
SELECT ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN rtg=5 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/COUNT(*),2), COUNT(*)
FROM mi_reviews WHERE uid IN (SELECT uid FROM repeat_users);"

task: For verified electronics reviews, how much longer on average (in characters) are 1-star review bodies compared to 5-star review bodies? Round to 1 decimal place. Only count reviews with non-empty body text.

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
SELECT rtg, COUNT(*), AVG(LENGTH(body))
FROM (
  SELECT f.rtg, f.body FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND f.vrf=1 AND f.body IS NOT NULL AND TRIM(f.body)<>''
  UNION ALL
  SELECT f.rtg, f.body FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND f.vrf='true' AND f.body IS NOT NULL AND TRIM(f.body)<>''
  UNION ALL
  SELECT f.rtg, f.body FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND f.vrf=1 AND f.body IS NOT NULL AND TRIM(f.body)<>''
)
WHERE rtg IN (1.0,5.0)
GROUP BY rtg;"

task: Among musical instrument brands with at least 20 products (using the attributes data), which brand has the highest average review rating? Report the brand name.

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
SELECT a.attr_val AS brand, COUNT(*) AS n, AVG(i.avg_rtg) AS avg_r
FROM attrs_g3 a
JOIN items_g3 i ON a.ref_id = i.ref_id
WHERE a.attr_key='Brand' AND i.main_cat='Musical Instruments' AND i.avg_rtg IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY a.attr_val
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 20
ORDER BY avg_r DESC
LIMIT 10;"

task: Among musical instrument brands with at least 20 products (using the attributes data), which brand has the highest average review rating? Report the brand name.

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
SELECT a.attr_val AS brand, COUNT(DISTINCT i.ref_id) AS n_prod, AVG(f.rtg) AS avg_review
FROM attrs_g3 a
JOIN items_g3 i ON a.ref_id = i.ref_id
JOIN fdbk_g3 f ON f.ref_id = i.ref_id
WHERE a.attr_key='Brand' AND i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'
GROUP BY a.attr_val
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT i.ref_id) >= 20
ORDER BY avg_review DESC
LIMIT 10;"

task: For how many office products does the stored average rating differ from the actual mean of its review scores by more than 1 full star?

arguments.command: sqlite3 database.db "SELECT MIN(rtg), MAX(rtg), AVG(rtg) FROM fdbk_g1; SELECT MIN(rtg), MAX(rtg), AVG(rtg) FROM fdbk_g2; SELECT MIN(rtg), MAX(rtg), AVG(rtg) FROM fdbk_g3;"

task: For how many office products does the stored average rating differ from the actual mean of its review scores by more than 1 full star?

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH op AS (
  SELECT ref_id, avg_rtg FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='Office Products'
  UNION ALL SELECT ref_id, avg_rtg FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='Office Products'
  UNION ALL SELECT ref_id, avg_rtg FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='Office Products'
),
f AS (
  SELECT ref_id, rtg FROM fdbk_g1
  UNION ALL SELECT ref_id, rtg FROM fdbk_g2
  UNION ALL SELECT ref_id, rtg FROM fdbk_g3
)
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (
  SELECT op.ref_id, op.avg_rtg, AVG(f.rtg) AS actual
  FROM op JOIN f ON op.ref_id = f.ref_id
  GROUP BY op.ref_id
  HAVING ABS(op.avg_rtg - actual) > 1.0
);"

task: How many office products have at least one review in the database but are NOT represented in the attributes data at all?

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
-- g1: office products with at least one review but not in attrs_g1
SELECT 'g1', COUNT(*) FROM (
  SELECT i.ref_id FROM items_g1 i
  WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products'
    AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM fdbk_g1 f WHERE f.ref_id=i.ref_id)
    AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM attrs_g1 a WHERE a.ref_id=i.ref_id)
);
-- g2: no attrs table exists, so all g2 office items with reviews qualify
SELECT 'g2', COUNT(*) FROM (
  SELECT i.ref_id FROM items_g2 i
  WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products'
    AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM fdbk_g2 f WHERE f.ref_id=i.ref_id)
);
-- g3
SELECT 'g3', COUNT(*) FROM (
  SELECT i.ref_id FROM items_g3 i
  WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products'
    AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM fdbk_g3 f WHERE f.ref_id=i.ref_id)
    AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM attrs_g3 a WHERE a.ref_id=i.ref_id)
);
"

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
-- For g1: normalize ts. Standard is ms (13 digits). 15 digits means microseconds.
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id 
WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' 
AND (
  (LENGTH(CAST(f.ts AS TEXT))=13 AND f.ts >= 1633046400000 AND f.ts < 1640995200000)
  OR (LENGTH(CAST(f.ts AS TEXT))=15 AND f.ts/1000 >= 1633046400000 AND f.ts/1000 < 1640995200000)
);
"

task: How many electronics product reviews were posted in Q4 (October through December) of 2021?

WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics'
AND (
  (LENGTH(CAST(f.ts AS TEXT))=10 AND f.ts >= 1633046400 AND f.ts < 1640995200)
  OR (LENGTH(CAST(f.ts AS TEXT))=12 AND f.ts/1000 >= 1633046400 AND f.ts/1000 < 1640995200)
);
"

task: How many electronics products priced above $50 have at least 5 reviews and an average review rating below 3.0?

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH g1 AS (
  SELECT i.ref_id FROM items_g1 i
  JOIN fdbk_g1 f ON f.ref_id = i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND i.prc > 50
  GROUP BY i.ref_id
  HAVING COUNT(f.id) >= 5 AND AVG(f.rtg) < 3.0
),
g2 AS (
  SELECT i.ref_id FROM items_g2 i
  JOIN fdbk_g2 f ON f.ref_id = i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND i.prc_usd > 50
  GROUP BY i.ref_id
  HAVING COUNT(f.id) >= 5 AND AVG(f.rtg) < 3.0
),
g3 AS (
  SELECT i.ref_id FROM items_g3 i
  JOIN fdbk_g3 f ON f.ref_id = i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND i.prc > 50
  GROUP BY i.ref_id
  HAVING COUNT(f.id) >= 5 AND AVG(f.rtg) < 3.0
)
SELECT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM g1) + (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM g2) + (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM g3);
"

sqlite3 database.db "
WITH mi AS (
  SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', datetime(f.ts/1000, 'unixepoch')) ym FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', datetime(f.ts, 'unixepoch')) ym FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT substr(f.ts,1,7) ym FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'
)
SELECT ym, COUNT(*) c FROM mi GROUP BY ym ORDER BY c DESC LIMIT 10;"

task: For musical instruments, how much higher (in star rating) is the average review rating for products with a listed price compared to products without a price? Round to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command: sqlite3 database.db "SELECT prc, avg_rtg FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments' LIMIT 10; SELECT '---'; SELECT prc, prc_usd, avg_rtg FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments' LIMIT 10; SELECT '---'; SELECT prc, avg_rtg FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments' LIMIT 10;"

task: For musical instruments, how much higher (in star rating) is the average review rating for products with a listed price compared to products without a price? Round to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
SELECT 
  (SELECT AVG(avg_rtg) FROM (
    SELECT avg_rtg, prc FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments'
    UNION ALL
    SELECT avg_rtg, prc FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments'
    UNION ALL
    SELECT avg_rtg, prc FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  ) WHERE prc IS NOT NULL AND avg_rtg IS NOT NULL) AS with_price,
  (SELECT AVG(avg_rtg) FROM (
    SELECT avg_rtg, prc FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments'
    UNION ALL
    SELECT avg_rtg, prc FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments'
    UNION ALL
    SELECT avg_rtg, prc FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  ) WHERE prc IS NULL AND avg_rtg IS NOT NULL) AS without_price;
"

task: What fraction of musical instrument reviews from repeat purchasers (users who have left 3 or more reviews in this category) are 5-star? Express as a percentage rounded to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH mi_reviews AS (
  SELECT f.uid, f.rtg FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT f.uid, f.rtg FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT f.uid, f.rtg FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'
),
repeat_users AS (
  SELECT uid FROM mi_reviews GROUP BY uid HAVING COUNT(*) >= 3
)
SELECT COUNT(*) total, SUM(CASE WHEN rtg=5.0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) five_star,
ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN rtg=5.0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/COUNT(*), 2)
FROM mi_reviews WHERE uid IN (SELECT uid FROM repeat_users);"

task: How many office product reviews have timestamps that correspond to dates in the year 2031 or later?

arguments.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import sqlite3, datetime
con=sqlite3.connect("database.db")
c=con.cursor()

def yr_ms(v):
    try: return datetime.datetime.utcfromtimestamp(v/1000).year
    except: return None
def yr_s(v):
    try: return datetime.datetime.utcfromtimestamp(v).year
    except: return None

# g1 office, ts is ms
c.execute("SELECT f.ts FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products'")
g1=sum(1 for (t,) in c if t is not None and (yr_ms(t) or 0)>=2031)

c.execute("SELECT f.ts FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products'")
g2=sum(1 for (t,) in c if t is not None and (yr_s(t) or 0)>=2031)

c.execute("SELECT f.ts FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products'")
g3=0
for (t,) in c:
    if t and len(str(t))>=4 and str(t)[:4].isdigit() and int(str(t)[:4])>=2031:
        g3+=1
print("g1",g1,"g2",g2,"g3",g3,"total",g1+g2+g3)
EOF

task: How many office product reviews have timestamps that correspond to dates in the year 2031 or later?

arguments.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import sqlite3, datetime
con=sqlite3.connect("database.db")
c=con.cursor()
def yr_ms(v):
    try: return datetime.datetime.utcfromtimestamp(v/1000).year
    except: return None
def yr_s(v):
    try: return datetime.datetime.utcfromtimestamp(v).year
    except: return None
from collections import Counter
c.execute("SELECT f.ts FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products'")
yrs=Counter(yr_ms(t) for (t,) in c if t)
print("g1 years>=2020:", {y:n for y,n in yrs.items() if y and y>=2020})
c.execute("SELECT f.ts FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products'")
yrs=Counter(yr_s(t) for (t,) in c if t)
print("g2 years>=2020:", {y:n for y,n in yrs.items() if y and y>=2020})
c.execute("SELECT f.ts FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products'")
yrs=Counter(int(str(t)[:4]) for (t,) in c if t and str(t)[:4].isdigit())
print("g3 years>=2020:", {y:n for y,n in yrs.items() if y>=2020})
EOF

task: How many office product reviews have timestamps that correspond to dates in the year 2031 or later?

arguments.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import sqlite3, datetime
con=sqlite3.connect("database.db"); c=con.cursor()
# naive: g1 ts as ms, g2 ts as s. year>=2031, allow huge years
def yr(v, div):
    # compute year from epoch/div seconds without overflow
    days = (v/div)/86400
    return 1970 + days/365.25
c.execute("SELECT f.ts FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products'")
g1=sum(1 for (t,) in c if t and yr(t,1000)>=2031)
c.execute("SELECT f.ts FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products'")
g2=sum(1 for (t,) in c if t and yr(t,1)>=2031)
c.execute("SELECT f.ts FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products'")
g3=sum(1 for (t,) in c if t and str(t)[:4].isdigit() and int(str(t)[:4])>=2031)
print("g1",g1,"g2",g2,"g3",g3,"total",g1+g2+g3)
EOF

task: For how many office products does the stored average rating differ from the actual mean of its review scores by more than 1 full star?

arguments.command: sqlite3 database.db "SELECT MIN(avg_rtg), MAX(avg_rtg) FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='Office Products'; SELECT MIN(avg_rtg), MAX(avg_rtg) FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='Office Products'; SELECT MIN(avg_rtg), MAX(avg_rtg) FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='Office Products';"

task: For how many office products does the stored average rating differ from the actual mean of its review scores by more than 1 full star?

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH combined AS (
  SELECT i.ref_id, i.avg_rtg, f.rtg FROM items_g1 i JOIN fdbk_g1 f ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT i.ref_id, i.avg_rtg, f.rtg FROM items_g2 i JOIN fdbk_g2 f ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT i.ref_id, i.avg_rtg, f.rtg FROM items_g3 i JOIN fdbk_g3 f ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products'
),
agg AS (
  SELECT ref_id, avg_rtg, AVG(rtg) as actual FROM combined GROUP BY ref_id, avg_rtg
)
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM agg WHERE ABS(avg_rtg - actual) > 1.0;
"

task: For how many office products does the stored average rating differ from the actual mean of its review scores by more than 1 full star?

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH g1 AS (SELECT i.ref_id, i.avg_rtg, AVG(f.rtg) actual FROM items_g1 i JOIN fdbk_g1 f ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products' GROUP BY i.ref_id),
g2 AS (SELECT i.ref_id, i.avg_rtg, AVG(f.rtg) actual FROM items_g2 i JOIN fdbk_g2 f ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products' GROUP BY i.ref_id),
g3 AS (SELECT i.ref_id, i.avg_rtg, AVG(f.rtg) actual FROM items_g3 i JOIN fdbk_g3 f ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products' GROUP BY i.ref_id)
SELECT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM g1 WHERE ABS(avg_rtg-actual)>1.0),
       (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM g2 WHERE ABS(avg_rtg-actual)>1.0),
       (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM g3 WHERE ABS(avg_rtg-actual)>1.0);
"

task: How many electronics products priced above $50 have at least 5 reviews and an average review rating below 3.0?
How many electronics products priced above $50 have at least 5 reviews and an average review rating below 3.0?

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH e AS (
  SELECT ref_id, prc AS price FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='All Electronics'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT ref_id, prc_usd AS price FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='All Electronics'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT ref_id, prc AS price FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='All Electronics'
),
f AS (
  SELECT ref_id, rtg FROM fdbk_g1
  UNION ALL
  SELECT ref_id, rtg FROM fdbk_g2
  UNION ALL
  SELECT ref_id, rtg FROM fdbk_g3
)
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (
  SELECT e.ref_id, e.price, COUNT(f.rtg) AS ct, AVG(f.rtg) AS ar
  FROM e JOIN f ON e.ref_id=f.ref_id
  WHERE e.price > 50
  GROUP BY e.ref_id, e.price
  HAVING COUNT(f.rtg) >= 5 AND AVG(f.rtg) < 3.0
);"

task: How many office product reviews were posted in calendar year 2022 for products whose catalog list price is strictly between $20 and $200 (exclude products priced exactly at those boundaries)?

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
SELECT
(SELECT COUNT(*) FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products' AND i.prc>20 AND i.prc<200
  AND strftime('%Y', f.ts/1000, 'unixepoch')='2022') AS g1,
(SELECT COUNT(*) FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products'
  AND COALESCE(i.prc_usd, i.prc/100.0)>20 AND COALESCE(i.prc_usd, i.prc/100.0)<200
  AND strftime('%Y', f.ts, 'unixepoch')='2022') AS g2,
(SELECT COUNT(*) FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products' AND i.prc>20 AND i.prc<200
  AND substr(f.ts,1,4)='2022') AS g3;
"

task: What percentage of office product reviewers have left reviews on products spanning 2 or more different top-level categories? Round to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH um AS (
  SELECT f.uid AS uid, i.main_cat AS cat FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  UNION ALL
  SELECT f.uid, i.main_cat FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  UNION ALL
  SELECT f.uid, i.main_cat FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
),
office_reviewers AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT uid FROM um WHERE cat='Office Products'
),
per_user AS (
  SELECT u.uid, COUNT(DISTINCT um.cat) AS ncat
  FROM office_reviewers u JOIN um ON u.uid=um.uid
  WHERE um.cat IS NOT NULL AND um.cat <> ''
  GROUP BY u.uid
)
SELECT 
  COUNT(*) AS total_office_reviewers,
  SUM(CASE WHEN ncat>=2 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS span2plus,
  ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN ncat>=2 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/COUNT(*),2) AS pct
FROM per_user;"

task: Among electronics reviews with a review body longer than 500 characters, considering only products whose list price is at most $40, what percentage are 1-star? Round to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH r AS (
  SELECT f.rtg AS rtg FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
    WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND i.prc IS NOT NULL AND i.prc<=40 AND f.body IS NOT NULL AND LENGTH(f.body)>500
  UNION ALL
  SELECT f.rtg FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
    WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND i.prc_usd IS NOT NULL AND i.prc_usd<=40 AND f.body IS NOT NULL AND LENGTH(f.body)>500
  UNION ALL
  SELECT f.rtg FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
    WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND i.prc IS NOT NULL AND i.prc<=40 AND f.body IS NOT NULL AND LENGTH(f.body)>500
)
SELECT COUNT(*), SUM(CASE WHEN rtg=1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END),
ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN rtg=1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/COUNT(*),2) FROM r;"

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH prods AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT i.ref_id, i.prc/100.0 AS price
  FROM items_g2 i
  JOIN taxn_g2 t ON t.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE t.cat_lvl=1 AND t.cat_nm='Computers & Accessories'
    AND i.prc IS NOT NULL AND i.prc>0
),
a AS (SELECT AVG(price) avgp FROM prods)
SELECT COUNT(*) total, SUM(CASE WHEN price>(SELECT avgp FROM a) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) above,
ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN price>(SELECT avgp FROM a) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/COUNT(*),2) pct,
(SELECT avgp FROM a) avgprice
FROM prods;"

task: For verified electronics reviews, how much longer on average (in characters) are 1-star review bodies compared to 5-star review bodies? Round to 1 decimal place. Only count reviews with non-empty body text.

arguments.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import sqlite3
con = sqlite3.connect("database.db")
cur = con.cursor()

def compute(cat_filter):
    tot1_len=tot1_ct=tot5_len=tot5_ct=0
    for g in [1,2,3]:
        vrf = "vrf='true'" if g==2 else "vrf=1"
        q=f"""
        SELECT f.rtg, LENGTH(f.body)
        FROM fdbk_g{g} f JOIN items_g{g} i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
        WHERE {vrf} AND {cat_filter}
          AND f.body IS NOT NULL AND TRIM(f.body)<>''
          AND f.rtg IN (1.0,5.0)
        """
        for rtg,l in cur.execute(q):
            if rtg==1.0: tot1_len+=l; tot1_ct+=1
            else: tot5_len+=l; tot5_ct+=1
    a1=tot1_len/tot1_ct
    a5=tot5_len/tot5_ct
    return a1,a5,a1-a5,tot1_ct,tot5_ct

print("All Electronics only:", compute("i.main_cat='All Electronics'"))
print("main_cat LIKE %Electronics%:", compute("i.main_cat LIKE '%Electronics%'"))
EOF

task: What is the average review rating given to office products that cost more than $50?

arguments.command: sqlite3 database.db "SELECT MIN(rtg), MAX(rtg), COUNT(*) FROM fdbk_g1; SELECT MIN(rtg), MAX(rtg), COUNT(*) FROM fdbk_g2; SELECT MIN(rtg), MAX(rtg), COUNT(*) FROM fdbk_g3;"

task: What is the average review rating given to office products that cost more than $50?

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
SELECT 
  (SELECT SUM(rtg) FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products' AND i.prc>50) +
  (SELECT SUM(rtg) FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products' AND i.prc_usd>50) +
  (SELECT SUM(rtg) FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products' AND i.prc>50) AS total_sum,
  (SELECT COUNT(rtg) FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products' AND i.prc>50) +
  (SELECT COUNT(rtg) FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products' AND i.prc_usd>50) +
  (SELECT COUNT(rtg) FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products' AND i.prc>50) AS total_ct;
"

task: For how many office products does the stored average rating differ from the actual mean of its review scores by more than 1 full star?

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH g1 AS (
  SELECT i.ref_id, i.avg_rtg AS stored, AVG(f.rtg) AS actual
  FROM items_g1 i JOIN fdbk_g1 f ON i.ref_id=f.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products'
  GROUP BY i.ref_id
),
g2 AS (
  SELECT i.ref_id, i.avg_rtg AS stored, AVG(f.rtg) AS actual
  FROM items_g2 i JOIN fdbk_g2 f ON i.ref_id=f.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products'
  GROUP BY i.ref_id
),
g3 AS (
  SELECT i.ref_id, i.avg_rtg AS stored, AVG(f.rtg) AS actual
  FROM items_g3 i JOIN fdbk_g3 f ON i.ref_id=f.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products'
  GROUP BY i.ref_id
),
u AS (SELECT * FROM g1 UNION ALL SELECT * FROM g2 UNION ALL SELECT * FROM g3)
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM u WHERE ABS(stored - actual) > 1;
"

task: For how many office products does the stored average rating differ from the actual mean of its review scores by more than 1 full star?
For how many office products does the stored average rating differ from the actual mean of its review scores by more than 1 full star?

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH g1 AS (
  SELECT i.ref_id, i.avg_rtg AS stored, AVG(f.rtg) AS actual, COUNT(*) c
  FROM items_g1 i JOIN fdbk_g1 f ON i.ref_id=f.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products'
  GROUP BY i.ref_id
),
g2 AS (
  SELECT i.ref_id, i.avg_rtg AS stored, AVG(f.rtg) AS actual, COUNT(*) c
  FROM items_g2 i JOIN fdbk_g2 f ON i.ref_id=f.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products'
  GROUP BY i.ref_id
),
g3 AS (
  SELECT i.ref_id, i.avg_rtg AS stored, AVG(f.rtg) AS actual, COUNT(*) c
  FROM items_g3 i JOIN fdbk_g3 f ON i.ref_id=f.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products'
  GROUP BY i.ref_id
)
SELECT 'g1', SUM(CASE WHEN ABS(stored-actual)>1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), COUNT(*) FROM g1
UNION ALL
SELECT 'g2', SUM(CASE WHEN ABS(stored-actual)>1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), COUNT(*) FROM g2
UNION ALL
SELECT 'g3', SUM(CASE WHEN ABS(stored-actual)>1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), COUNT(*) FROM g3;
"

task: How many office products have at least one review in the database but are NOT represented in the attributes data at all?

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
SELECT 'g1_with_review_no_attr', COUNT(DISTINCT i.ref_id) FROM items_g1 i 
WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products' 
AND EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM fdbk_g1 f WHERE f.ref_id=i.ref_id)
AND NOT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM attrs_g1 a WHERE a.ref_id=i.ref_id);

SELECT 'g3_with_review_no_attr', COUNT(DISTINCT i.ref_id) FROM items_g3 i 
WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products' 
AND EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM fdbk_g3 f WHERE f.ref_id=i.ref_id)
AND NOT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM attrs_g3 a WHERE a.ref_id=i.ref_id);

SELECT 'g2_with_review', COUNT(DISTINCT i.ref_id) FROM items_g2 i 
WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products' 
AND EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM fdbk_g2 f WHERE f.ref_id=i.ref_id);
"

arguments.command: sqlite3 database.db "SELECT MIN(rtg), MAX(rtg) FROM fdbk_g1; SELECT MIN(rtg), MAX(rtg) FROM fdbk_g2; SELECT MIN(rtg), MAX(rtg) FROM fdbk_g3;"

task: How many electronics products priced above $50 have at least 5 reviews and an average review rating below 3.0?

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH g1 AS (
  SELECT i.ref_id, i.prc AS price, COUNT(f.id) AS rc, AVG(f.rtg) AS ar
  FROM items_g1 i LEFT JOIN fdbk_g1 f ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND i.prc IS NOT NULL AND i.prc>50
  GROUP BY i.ref_id
),
g2 AS (
  SELECT i.ref_id, i.prc_usd AS price, COUNT(f.id) AS rc, AVG(f.rtg) AS ar
  FROM items_g2 i LEFT JOIN fdbk_g2 f ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND i.prc_usd IS NOT NULL AND i.prc_usd>50
  GROUP BY i.ref_id
),
g3 AS (
  SELECT i.ref_id, i.prc AS price, COUNT(f.id) AS rc, AVG(f.rtg) AS ar
  FROM items_g3 i LEFT JOIN fdbk_g3 f ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND i.prc IS NOT NULL AND i.prc>50
  GROUP BY i.ref_id
)
SELECT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM g1 WHERE rc>=5 AND ar<3.0)
     + (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM g2 WHERE rc>=5 AND ar<3.0)
     + (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM g3 WHERE rc>=5 AND ar<3.0);
"

observation.content:
1|B08L4K4QKR|B08L4K4QKR|AFKZENTNBQ7A7V7UXW5JJI6UGRYQ|3.0|Could have been wonderful. Has execution flaws.|Update: received replacement & the second is just like the first unfortunately. So I will assume this was made by the same crafter & from the same batch as the last. Again, the paper has been pulled too tightly & ripped through the backing. It has also been cut through the first 30% of the pages at one edge. There are paint pen marking all over the journal inside & out. This one has a cut down the front where maybe they had begun to make a scoring cut in the leather & realized it was going to be too short & then just put the design over it. They did a better job of getting a deeper embossing on this one tho. The finish is worse however. This one has a weird finish almost like pumice stones where it looks kind of crackled & porous Idk if it was done on purpose as a design or not. I wish this crafter would learn to not use paint pens to mark up leather bc it ruins the product. If you try to remove the paint pen you ruin the leather. I am double bummed for having given them a second chance.
Old review:
This could have been a beautiful little hotter notebook. Unfortunately it is not. The ppl that made it decided to use metallic paint pens & permanent markers to mark all their sewing lines ruining the aesthetic. There is not a spot on either side without a blemish. I had resolved myself that if only the inside was ugly I could live with that. Lol. Bc there’s a lot of that irl imo, but it turned out that both the inside & outside had imperfections behind what I was prepared to pay for & live with. As a disabled person I can generally live with a lot lol, but I spend my little bit of money on writing tools & I like them to be nice. This could have been a thing of true beauty as it reminds me of several Levenger notebooks I own. Sadly, while the idea was great the execution is lacking. I hope they will do it right next time bc it’s the perfect size to throw into a purse or backpack for a short trip or even a longer jaunt if you bring paper refills. The paper quality was not what I was hoping for either & as it arrived torn & already pulling away from its base I would not have used it anyways. As it is it is a hard no for me in its current state. I hope that either the seller or amazon will do right me. Someone should definitely be doing quality inspection checks bc this would not have passed even a rudimentary one. The metallic silver paint pen was a glaring no staring me right in the face the second I opened it up. The black permanent marker while it did match the journal better was still extremely obvious & still extremely annoying to see. It’s a well thought out design in my estimation just an extremely flawed execution. I hope the seller tweaks their issues & offers it in other colors bc I would totally try again.|1643160661334|0|1 2|B000KJRRBQ|B082XWH5V6|AGGZ357AO26RQZVRLGU4D4N52DZQ|4.0|Sturdy key and mail rack|Other than a slight design flaw that really warranted an exchange (the bottom mail "shelf" is welded to the frame at a little bit of an angle, causing the key hooks to also have a slight angle as well), this rack works great. I didn't feel like going through the effort of exchanging it, so I kept the one I received. It fits a lot of stuff without being too bulky on the wall (hubby said nothing wooden - that looks too "granny"!) Note that it does not come with metal plaster anchors, just screws, so you may need to make a trip to your local hardware store for some additional supplies if you have plaster or drywall. Overall, pleased with the product compared to others available for purchase.|1341922802000|0|1 3|B07LFTC5HV|B07LFVKTV1|AG6BAEKWLCWH2TW3KKLVK773YF6A|4.0|good quality for the price. fat man recommended|The wheel size mimics an inline hockey goaltender wheel which effectively lowers your chair between 1/2 to 1 full inch.

Sturdy for the price, but don't expect skate worthy bearings

Whole unit comes dry from the factory so if you have a high quality lube, use it.

Good buy. I got two sets. 1 black and 1 blue. Too bad red is not available.|1610671050277|0|1

task: In which year-month were the most musical instrument reviews posted? Report as YYYY-MM (e.g. '2020-01').

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
SELECT ym, COUNT(*) c FROM (
  SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', ts/1000, 'unixepoch') ym FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i USING(ref_id) WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', ts, 'unixepoch') ym FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i USING(ref_id) WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT substr(ts,1,7) ym FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i USING(ref_id) WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'
) GROUP BY ym ORDER BY c DESC LIMIT 10;"

task: For musical instruments, how much higher (in star rating) is the average review rating for products with a listed price compared to products without a price? Round to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command: sqlite3 database.db "SELECT COUNT(*), COUNT(prc), SUM(CASE WHEN prc IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments'; SELECT '---'; SELECT COUNT(*), COUNT(prc), COUNT(prc_usd) FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments'; SELECT '---'; SELECT COUNT(*), COUNT(prc) FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments';"

task: For musical instruments, how much higher (in star rating) is the average review rating for products with a listed price compared to products without a price? Round to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command: sqlite3 database.db "SELECT prc, avg_rtg FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments' LIMIT 20; SELECT '---g2'; SELECT prc, prc_usd, avg_rtg FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments' LIMIT 20; SELECT '---g3'; SELECT prc, avg_rtg FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments' LIMIT 20;"

task: For musical instruments, how much higher (in star rating) is the average review rating for products with a listed price compared to products without a price? Round to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH combined AS (
  SELECT prc, avg_rtg FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT prc_usd, avg_rtg FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT prc, avg_rtg FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments'
)
SELECT 
  AVG(CASE WHEN prc IS NOT NULL THEN avg_rtg END) AS with_price,
  AVG(CASE WHEN prc IS NULL THEN avg_rtg END) AS without_price,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN prc IS NOT NULL THEN 1 END) AS n_with,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN prc IS NULL THEN 1 END) AS n_without
FROM combined WHERE avg_rtg IS NOT NULL;"

task: How many office products have reviews spanning 5 or more distinct calendar years? Exclude reviews with corrupted timestamps (dates in 2031 or later).

task: How many office products have reviews spanning 5 or more distinct calendar years? Exclude reviews with corrupted timestamps (dates in 2031 or later).

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH g1c AS (
  SELECT f.ref_id, CAST(strftime('%Y', f.ts/1000, 'unixepoch') AS INTEGER) AS yr
  FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products'
),
g2c AS (
  SELECT f.ref_id, CAST(strftime('%Y', f.ts, 'unixepoch') AS INTEGER) AS yr
  FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products'
),
g3c AS (
  SELECT f.ref_id, CAST(substr(f.ts,1,4) AS INTEGER) AS yr
  FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products' AND f.ts IS NOT NULL
),
all_c AS (
  SELECT ref_id, yr FROM g1c WHERE yr<2031
  UNION ALL SELECT ref_id, yr FROM g2c WHERE yr<2031
  UNION ALL SELECT ref_id, yr FROM g3c WHERE yr<2031
)
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT ref_id, COUNT(DISTINCT yr) d FROM all_c GROUP BY ref_id HAVING d>=5);
"

task: What fraction of musical instrument reviews from repeat purchasers (users who have left 3 or more reviews in this category) are 5-star? Express as a percentage rounded to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH mi_reviews AS (
  SELECT f.uid, f.rtg FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT f.uid, f.rtg FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT f.uid, f.rtg FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'
),
repeat_users AS (
  SELECT uid FROM mi_reviews GROUP BY uid HAVING COUNT(*) >= 3
)
SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN rtg=5.0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS five, COUNT(*) AS total,
       ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN rtg=5.0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/COUNT(*), 2) AS pct
FROM mi_reviews WHERE uid IN (SELECT uid FROM repeat_users);
"

task: What fraction of musical instrument reviews from repeat purchasers (users who have left 3 or more reviews in this category) are 5-star? Express as a percentage rounded to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH mi_reviews AS (
  SELECT f.uid, f.rtg FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'
),
repeat_users AS (
  SELECT uid FROM mi_reviews GROUP BY uid HAVING COUNT(*) >= 3
)
SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN rtg=5.0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS five, COUNT(*) AS total,
       ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN rtg=5.0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/COUNT(*), 4) AS pct
FROM mi_reviews WHERE uid IN (SELECT uid FROM repeat_users);
"

arguments.command:

echo "g2:"; sqlite3 database.db "

echo "g3:"; sqlite3 database.db "
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products' AND i.prc>20 AND i.prc<200
AND substr(f.ts,1,4)='2022';"

task: What percentage of office product reviewers have left reviews on products spanning 2 or more different top-level categories? Round to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH rc AS (
  SELECT f.uid AS uid, i.main_cat AS cat FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  UNION ALL
  SELECT f.uid, i.main_cat FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  UNION ALL
  SELECT f.uid, i.main_cat FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
),
per AS (
  SELECT uid,
         COUNT(DISTINCT cat) AS ncat,
         MAX(CASE WHEN cat='Office Products' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS has_office
  FROM rc
  WHERE cat IS NOT NULL AND cat<>''
  GROUP BY uid
)
SELECT COUNT(*) AS office_reviewers,
       SUM(CASE WHEN ncat>=2 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS multi,
       ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN ncat>=2 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/COUNT(*),2) AS pct
FROM per WHERE has_office=1;
"

task: Among electronics reviews with a review body longer than 500 characters, considering only products whose list price is at most $40, what percentage are 1-star? Round to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
SELECT 
  SUM(CASE WHEN f.rtg=1.0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as onestar,
  COUNT(*) as total
FROM (
  SELECT f.rtg FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
    WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND i.prc<=40 AND i.prc IS NOT NULL AND LENGTH(f.body)>500
  UNION ALL
  SELECT f.rtg FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
    WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND i.prc_usd<=40 AND i.prc_usd IS NOT NULL AND LENGTH(f.body)>500
  UNION ALL
  SELECT f.rtg FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
    WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND i.prc<=40 AND i.prc IS NOT NULL AND LENGTH(f.body)>500
) f;
"

task: For verified electronics reviews, how much longer on average (in characters) are 1-star review bodies compared to 5-star review bodies? Round to 1 decimal place. Only count reviews with non-empty body text.

arguments.command:
python3 -c "
import sqlite3
c=sqlite3.connect('database.db')
def q(g,rtg):
    v=\"f.vrf='true'\" if g==2 else 'f.vrf=1'
    cur=c.execute(f\"SELECT SUM(LENGTH(f.body)), COUNT(*) FROM fdbk_g{g} f JOIN items_g{g} i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND {v} AND f.body IS NOT NULL AND TRIM(f.body)<>'' AND f.rtg={rtg}\")
    return cur.fetchone()
s1=t1=s5=t5=0
for g in (1,2,3):
    a,b=q(g,1.0); s1+=a; t1+=b
    a,b=q(g,5.0); s5+=a; t5+=b
avg1=s1/t1; avg5=s5/t5
print('avg1',avg1,'avg5',avg5,'diff',round(avg1-avg5,1))
"

task: How many electronics product reviews have timestamps that correspond to dates in the year 2031 or later?

arguments.command:
# Normalize: g1 ms(13)->/1000, ms(15)->/100000 to get sec; general: reduce to seconds
# g1: 13-digit -> /1000 sec ; 15-digit -> /100000? Let's just: if len 15 divide by 1000000... check
# 931911943000000: valid ms should be 931911943000 (13). So 15-digit /1000 = ms, then /1000 = sec => /1e6 sec
python3 <<'EOF'
import sqlite3, datetime
con=sqlite3.connect('database.db')
cur=con.cursor()
threshold=1924992000  # 2031-01-01 UTC in seconds

# electronics categories interpretation: main_cat = 'All Electronics'
cat="All Electronics"

def year_ge_2031_g1():
    cur.execute("SELECT f.ts FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat=?",(cat,))
    c=0
    for (ts,) in cur:
        if ts is None: continue
        L=len(str(ts))
        if L==13: sec=ts/1000
        elif L==15: sec=ts/100000  # /1000 ms then /1000? 15->13 removing 2 digits -> /100 => ms; ms/1000=sec => /100000
        else: sec=ts/1000
        if sec>=threshold: c+=1
    return c

def g2():
    cur.execute("SELECT f.ts FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat=?",(cat,))
    c=0
    for (ts,) in cur:
        if ts is None: continue
        L=len(str(ts))
        if L==10: sec=ts
        elif L==12: sec=ts/1000
        else: sec=ts
        if sec>=threshold: c+=1
    return c

def g3():
    cur.execute("SELECT f.ts FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat=?",(cat,))
    c=0
    for (ts,) in cur:
        if not ts: continue
        try:
            y=int(ts[:4])
        except: continue
        if y>=2031: c+=1
    return c

print("g1",year_ge_2031_g1())
print("g2",g2())
print("g3",g3())
EOF

task: How many electronics product reviews have timestamps that correspond to dates in the year 2031 or later?

arguments.command:
python3 <<'EOF'
import sqlite3, datetime
con=sqlite3.connect('database.db')
cur=con.cursor()
# check the 51 g1 hits: what lengths and dates
cur.execute("SELECT f.ts FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics'")
from collections import Counter
lens=Counter()
hits=[]
for (ts,) in cur:
    if ts is None: continue
    L=len(str(ts))
    if L==15: sec=ts/100000
    else: sec=ts/1000
    if sec>=1924992000:
        hits.append((L,ts,datetime.datetime.utcfromtimestamp(sec).isoformat()))
        lens[L]+=1
print(lens)
for h in hits[:10]: print(h)
# also verify 15-digit conversion gives sensible dates overall
print("--- sample 15-digit conversions ---")
cur.execute("SELECT ts FROM fdbk_g1 WHERE length(CAST(ts AS TEXT))=15 LIMIT 5")
for (ts,) in cur:
    print(ts, datetime.datetime.utcfromtimestamp(ts/100000).isoformat())
EOF

task: How many electronics product reviews have timestamps that correspond to dates in the year 2031 or later?

arguments.command:
python3 <<'EOF'
import sqlite3, datetime
con=sqlite3.connect('database.db')
cur=con.cursor()
threshold=1924992000  # 2031-01-01 UTC seconds
cat="All Electronics"

def g1():
    cur.execute("SELECT f.ts FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat=?",(cat,))
    c=0
    for (ts,) in cur:
        if ts is None: continue
        L=len(str(ts))
        if L==13: sec=ts/1000
        elif L==15: sec=ts/1000000  # ms*1000
        else: sec=ts/1000
        if sec>=threshold: c+=1
    return c

def g2():
    cur.execute("SELECT f.ts FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat=?",(cat,))
    c=0
    for (ts,) in cur:
        if ts is None: continue
        L=len(str(ts))
        if L==10: sec=ts
        elif L==12: sec=ts/1000  # sec*1000
        else: sec=ts
        if sec>=threshold: c+=1
    return c

def g3():
    cur.execute("SELECT f.ts FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat=?",(cat,))
    c=0
    for (ts,) in cur:
        if not ts: continue
        try: y=int(ts[:4])
        except: continue
        if y>=2031: c+=1
    return c
a,b,c=g1(),g2(),g3()
print("g1",a,"g2",b,"g3",c,"total",a+b+c)
EOF

task: How many electronics product reviews have timestamps that correspond to dates in the year 2031 or later?

arguments.command:
python3 <<'EOF'
import sqlite3, datetime
con=sqlite3.connect('database.db')
cur=con.cursor()
threshold=1924992000
# ALL reviews normalized, count 2031+ per group and find max dates
def norm_g1(ts):
    L=len(str(ts))
    return ts/1000 if L==13 else (ts/1000000 if L==15 else ts/1000)
def norm_g2(ts):
    L=len(str(ts))
    return ts if L==10 else (ts/1000 if L==12 else ts)

cur.execute("SELECT ts FROM fdbk_g1")
g1=[norm_g1(t) for (t,) in cur if t is not None]
cur.execute("SELECT ts FROM fdbk_g2")
g2=[norm_g2(t) for (t,) in cur if t is not None]
print("g1 max", datetime.datetime.utcfromtimestamp(max(g1)).isoformat(), "count>=2031", sum(1 for x in g1 if x>=threshold))
print("g2 max", datetime.datetime.utcfromtimestamp(max(g2)).isoformat(), "count>=2031", sum(1 for x in g2 if x>=threshold))
cur.execute("SELECT MAX(ts) FROM fdbk_g3 WHERE ts!=''")
print("g3 max", cur.fetchone()[0])
EOF

task: For how many office products does the stored average rating differ from the actual mean of its review scores by more than 1 full star?

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH office_items AS (
  SELECT ref_id, avg_rtg FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='Office Products'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT ref_id, avg_rtg FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='Office Products'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT ref_id, avg_rtg FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='Office Products'
),
all_fdbk AS (
  SELECT ref_id, rtg FROM fdbk_g1
  UNION ALL
  SELECT ref_id, rtg FROM fdbk_g2
  UNION ALL
  SELECT ref_id, rtg FROM fdbk_g3
),
actual AS (
  SELECT ref_id, AVG(rtg) AS m FROM all_fdbk GROUP BY ref_id
)
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM office_items o JOIN actual a ON o.ref_id=a.ref_id WHERE ABS(o.avg_rtg - a.m) > 1.0;
"

task: How many electronics products priced above $50 have at least 5 reviews and an average review rating below 3.0?
How many electronics products priced above $50 have at least 5 reviews and an average review rating below 3.0?

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
-- G1: electronics products with price > 50, review count >=5, avg rtg < 3.0 based on fdbk
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (
  SELECT i.ref_id, AVG(f.rtg) as ar, COUNT(f.id) as ct
  FROM items_g1 i JOIN fdbk_g1 f ON i.ref_id = f.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat = 'All Electronics' AND i.prc > 50
  GROUP BY i.ref_id
  HAVING COUNT(f.id) >= 5 AND AVG(f.rtg) < 3.0
);"

task: In which year-month were the most musical instrument reviews posted? Report as YYYY-MM (e.g. '2020-01').

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
SELECT ym, SUM(c) AS total FROM (
  SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', ts/1000, 'unixepoch') AS ym, COUNT(*) AS c
  FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments' GROUP BY ym
  UNION ALL
  SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', ts, 'unixepoch') AS ym, COUNT(*) AS c
  FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments' GROUP BY ym
  UNION ALL
  SELECT substr(ts,1,7) AS ym, COUNT(*) AS c
  FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments' GROUP BY ym
) GROUP BY ym ORDER BY total DESC LIMIT 10;"

task: For musical instruments, how much higher (in star rating) is the average review rating for products with a listed price compared to products without a price? Round to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH combined AS (
  SELECT avg_rtg, prc FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT avg_rtg, prc FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT avg_rtg, prc FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments'
)
SELECT 
  AVG(CASE WHEN prc IS NOT NULL THEN avg_rtg END) as with_price,
  AVG(CASE WHEN prc IS NULL THEN avg_rtg END) as without_price,
  ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN prc IS NOT NULL THEN avg_rtg END) - AVG(CASE WHEN prc IS NULL THEN avg_rtg END), 2) as diff
FROM combined WHERE avg_rtg IS NOT NULL;"

task: For musical instruments, how much higher (in star rating) is the average review rating for products with a listed price compared to products without a price? Round to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
SELECT 
  AVG(CASE WHEN prc IS NOT NULL THEN avg_rtg END) as with_price,
  AVG(CASE WHEN prc IS NULL THEN avg_rtg END) as without_price,
  ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN prc IS NOT NULL THEN avg_rtg END) - AVG(CASE WHEN prc IS NULL THEN avg_rtg END), 2) as diff
FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments' AND avg_rtg IS NOT NULL;"

task: Across both product categories that store attribute data, how many total products are listed as discontinued by the manufacturer and have a catalog average star rating strictly below 3.0?

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
SELECT (
  SELECT COUNT(*) FROM items_g1 i
  JOIN attrs_g1 a ON a.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE lower(a.attr_key)='is discontinued by manufacturer'
    AND (a.attr_val='Yes' OR a.attr_val='True')
    AND i.avg_rtg < 3.0
) + (
  SELECT COUNT(*) FROM items_g3 i
  JOIN attrs_g3 a ON a.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE lower(a.attr_key)='is discontinued by manufacturer'
    AND (a.attr_val='Yes' OR a.attr_val='True')
    AND i.avg_rtg < 3.0
);"

task: Across both product categories that store attribute data, how many total products are listed as discontinued by the manufacturer and have a catalog average star rating strictly below 3.0?

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
SELECT 'g1', COUNT(DISTINCT i.ref_id) FROM items_g1 i
JOIN attrs_g1 a ON a.ref_id=i.ref_id
WHERE lower(a.attr_key)='is discontinued by manufacturer'
  AND (a.attr_val='Yes' OR a.attr_val='True')
  AND i.avg_rtg < 3.0;
SELECT 'g3', COUNT(DISTINCT i.ref_id) FROM items_g3 i
JOIN attrs_g3 a ON a.ref_id=i.ref_id
WHERE lower(a.attr_key)='is discontinued by manufacturer'
  AND (a.attr_val='Yes' OR a.attr_val='True')
  AND i.avg_rtg < 3.0;"

task: What fraction of musical instrument reviews from repeat purchasers (users who have left 3 or more reviews in this category) are 5-star? Express as a percentage rounded to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command: sqlite3 database.db "SELECT rtg, COUNT(*) FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments' GROUP BY rtg;"

task: What fraction of musical instrument reviews from repeat purchasers (users who have left 3 or more reviews in this category) are 5-star? Express as a percentage rounded to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command: sqlite3 database.db "WITH mi AS (SELECT f.uid, f.rtg FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'), repeat_users AS (SELECT uid FROM mi GROUP BY uid HAVING COUNT(*)>=3) SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN rtg=5.0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)*1.0/COUNT(*)*100, COUNT(*) FROM mi WHERE uid IN (SELECT uid FROM repeat_users);"

task: What fraction of musical instrument reviews from repeat purchasers (users who have left 3 or more reviews in this category) are 5-star? Express as a percentage rounded to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command: sqlite3 database.db "WITH mi AS (SELECT f.uid, f.rtg FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'), repeat_users AS (SELECT uid FROM mi GROUP BY uid HAVING COUNT(*)>=3) SELECT ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN rtg=5.0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)*100.0/COUNT(*), 2) FROM mi WHERE uid IN (SELECT uid FROM repeat_users);"

task: What is the median price of electronics products (in dollars)? Only consider products with a non-zero price. Round to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command:
python3 -c "
import sqlite3
c=sqlite3.connect('database.db').cursor()
prices=[]
for g,col in [(1,'prc'),(2,'prc_usd'),(3,'prc')]:
    c.execute(f\"SELECT {col} FROM items_g{g} WHERE main_cat='All Electronics' AND {col} IS NOT NULL AND {col}!='' AND {col}>0\")
    prices+=[r[0] for r in c.fetchall()]
prices.sort()
n=len(prices)
if n%2: med=prices[n//2]
else: med=(prices[n//2-1]+prices[n//2])/2
print('count',n,'median',round(med,2))
"

observation.content: count 2739 median 23.99

task: Among electronics reviews with a review body longer than 500 characters, considering only products whose list price is at most $40, what percentage are 1-star? Round to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH r AS (
  SELECT f.rtg FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
    WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND LENGTH(f.body)>500 AND i.prc IS NOT NULL AND i.prc<=40
  UNION ALL
  SELECT f.rtg FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
    WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND LENGTH(f.body)>500 AND i.prc_usd IS NOT NULL AND i.prc_usd<=40
  UNION ALL
  SELECT f.rtg FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
    WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND LENGTH(f.body)>500 AND i.prc IS NOT NULL AND i.prc<=40
)
SELECT COUNT(*) total, SUM(CASE WHEN rtg=1.0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) onestar,
ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN rtg=1.0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/COUNT(*),2) pct FROM r;
"

task: For verified electronics reviews, how much longer on average (in characters) are 1-star review bodies compared to 5-star review bodies? Round to 1 decimal place. Only count reviews with non-empty body text.

arguments.command:
python3 -c "
import sqlite3
con=sqlite3.connect('database.db')
cur=con.cursor()
groups=[('fdbk_g1','items_g1','1'),('fdbk_g2','items_g2','true'),('fdbk_g3','items_g3','1')]
tot={1:[0,0],5:[0,0]}  # rating -> [sum_len, count]
for f,i,vtrue in groups:
    q=f'''SELECT rtg, LENGTH(body) FROM {f} fb JOIN {i} it ON fb.ref_id=it.ref_id
          WHERE it.main_cat='All Electronics' AND fb.body IS NOT NULL AND TRIM(fb.body)<>''
          AND fb.vrf=? AND fb.rtg IN (1.0,5.0)'''
    for rtg,l in cur.execute(q,(vtrue,)):
        r=int(rtg)
        tot[r][0]+=l
        tot[r][1]+=1
avg1=tot[1][0]/tot[1][1]
avg5=tot[5][0]/tot[5][1]
print('n1',tot[1][1],'n5',tot[5][1])
print('avg1',avg1,'avg5',avg5)
print('diff',round(avg1-avg5,1))
"

task: For how many office products does the stored average rating differ from the actual mean of its review scores by more than 1 full star?

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH office AS (
  SELECT ref_id, avg_rtg FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='Office Products'
  UNION ALL SELECT ref_id, avg_rtg FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='Office Products'
  UNION ALL SELECT ref_id, avg_rtg FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='Office Products'
),
r AS (
  SELECT ref_id, rtg FROM fdbk_g1
  UNION ALL SELECT ref_id, rtg FROM fdbk_g2
  UNION ALL SELECT ref_id, rtg FROM fdbk_g3
)
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (
  SELECT o.ref_id, o.avg_rtg, AVG(r.rtg) AS m
  FROM office o JOIN r ON r.ref_id=o.ref_id
  GROUP BY o.ref_id
  HAVING ABS(o.avg_rtg - m) > 1.0
);"

task: How many office products have at least one review in the database but are NOT represented in the attributes data at all?

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
SELECT 'g1_office_with_review_no_attr', COUNT(DISTINCT i.ref_id)
FROM items_g1 i
WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products'
  AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM fdbk_g1 f WHERE f.ref_id=i.ref_id)
  AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM attrs_g1 a WHERE a.ref_id=i.ref_id);
SELECT 'g2_office_with_review', COUNT(DISTINCT i.ref_id)
FROM items_g2 i
WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products'
  AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM fdbk_g2 f WHERE f.ref_id=i.ref_id);
SELECT 'g3_office_with_review_no_attr', COUNT(DISTINCT i.ref_id)
FROM items_g3 i
WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products'
  AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM fdbk_g3 f WHERE f.ref_id=i.ref_id)
  AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM attrs_g3 a WHERE a.ref_id=i.ref_id);
"

observation.content:
g1_office_with_review_no_attr|188
g2_office_with_review|128
g3_office_with_review_no_attr|0

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH combined AS (
  SELECT substr(f.ts,1,7) AS ym FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments' AND f.ts IS NOT NULL AND f.ts != ''
  UNION ALL
  SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', f.ts/1000, 'unixepoch') FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', f.ts, 'unixepoch') FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'
)
SELECT ym, COUNT(*) c FROM combined GROUP BY ym ORDER BY c DESC LIMIT 5;"

task: For musical instruments, how much higher (in star rating) is the average review rating for products with a listed price compared to products without a price? Round to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command: sqlite3 database.db "SELECT prc, prc_usd FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments' LIMIT 10;"

task: For musical instruments, how much higher (in star rating) is the average review rating for products with a listed price compared to products without a price? Round to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command: sqlite3 database.db "SELECT prc, avg_rtg FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments';"

task: For musical instruments, how much higher (in star rating) is the average review rating for products with a listed price compared to products without a price? Round to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH combined AS (
  SELECT prc, avg_rtg FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT prc_usd AS prc, avg_rtg FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT prc, avg_rtg FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments'
)
SELECT 
  (SELECT AVG(avg_rtg) FROM combined WHERE prc IS NOT NULL AND avg_rtg IS NOT NULL) AS with_prc,
  (SELECT AVG(avg_rtg) FROM combined WHERE prc IS NULL AND avg_rtg IS NOT NULL) AS without_prc,
  (SELECT AVG(avg_rtg) FROM combined WHERE prc IS NOT NULL AND avg_rtg IS NOT NULL) - (SELECT AVG(avg_rtg) FROM combined WHERE prc IS NULL AND avg_rtg IS NOT NULL) AS diff;
"

task: For musical instruments, how much higher (in star rating) is the average review rating for products with a listed price compared to products without a price? Round to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH combined AS (
  SELECT prc, avg_rtg FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT prc_usd AS prc, avg_rtg FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT prc, avg_rtg FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments'
)
SELECT prc IS NOT NULL as has_prc, COUNT(*), AVG(avg_rtg) FROM combined WHERE avg_rtg IS NOT NULL GROUP BY has_prc;
"

task: For musical instruments, how much higher (in star rating) is the average review rating for products with a listed price compared to products without a price? Round to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command: sqlite3 database.db "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments' AND prc IS NULL AND prc_usd IS NOT NULL; SELECT COUNT(*) FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments' AND prc IS NOT NULL AND prc_usd IS NULL;"

task: For musical instruments, how much higher (in star rating) is the average review rating for products with a listed price compared to products without a price? Round to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command: sqlite3 database.db "SELECT prc, prc_usd, avg_rtg FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments' AND prc IS NOT NULL AND prc_usd IS NULL;"

task: For musical instruments, how much higher (in star rating) is the average review rating for products with a listed price compared to products without a price? Round to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH combined AS (
  SELECT (prc IS NOT NULL) as has_prc, avg_rtg FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT (prc IS NOT NULL OR prc_usd IS NOT NULL) as has_prc, avg_rtg FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT (prc IS NOT NULL) as has_prc, avg_rtg FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments'
)
SELECT has_prc, COUNT(*), AVG(avg_rtg) FROM combined WHERE avg_rtg IS NOT NULL GROUP BY has_prc;
"

task: Across both product categories that store attribute data, how many total products are listed as discontinued by the manufacturer and have a catalog average star rating strictly below 3.0?

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (
  SELECT a.ref_id FROM attrs_g1 a JOIN items_g1 i ON a.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE lower(a.attr_key)='is discontinued by manufacturer' AND a.attr_val IN ('Yes','True') AND i.avg_rtg < 3.0
  UNION ALL
  SELECT a.ref_id FROM attrs_g3 a JOIN items_g3 i ON a.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE lower(a.attr_key)='is discontinued by manufacturer' AND a.attr_val IN ('Yes','True') AND i.avg_rtg < 3.0
);"

task: How many office products have reviews spanning 5 or more distinct calendar years? Exclude reviews with corrupted timestamps (dates in 2031 or later).

task: How many office products have reviews spanning 5 or more distinct calendar years? Exclude reviews with corrupted timestamps (dates in 2031 or later).

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH office AS (
  SELECT ref_id FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='Office Products'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT ref_id FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='Office Products'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT ref_id FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='Office Products'
),
reviews AS (
  SELECT f.ref_id, CAST(strftime('%Y', f.ts/1000, 'unixepoch') AS INTEGER) AS yr FROM fdbk_g1 f WHERE f.ref_id IN (SELECT ref_id FROM office)
  UNION ALL
  SELECT f.ref_id, CAST(strftime('%Y', f.ts, 'unixepoch') AS INTEGER) FROM fdbk_g2 f WHERE f.ref_id IN (SELECT ref_id FROM office)
  UNION ALL
  SELECT f.ref_id, CAST(substr(f.ts,1,4) AS INTEGER) FROM fdbk_g3 f WHERE f.ref_id IN (SELECT ref_id FROM office)
)
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (
  SELECT ref_id FROM reviews WHERE yr < 2031 AND yr IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY ref_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT yr) >= 5
);
"

task: What fraction of musical instrument reviews from repeat purchasers (users who have left 3 or more reviews in this category) are 5-star? Express as a percentage rounded to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH mi_reviews AS (
  SELECT f.uid, f.rtg FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT f.uid, f.rtg FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT f.uid, f.rtg FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'
),
repeat_users AS (SELECT uid FROM mi_reviews GROUP BY uid HAVING COUNT(*)>=3)
SELECT COUNT(*) total, SUM(CASE WHEN r.rtg=5.0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) fives, ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN r.rtg=5.0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/COUNT(*),2) pct
FROM mi_reviews r JOIN repeat_users ru ON r.uid=ru.uid;"

task: How many office product reviews were posted in calendar year 2022 for products whose catalog list price is strictly between $20 and $200 (exclude products priced exactly at those boundaries)?

arguments.command:
# g1: normal ts ms -> /1000; corrupted 15-digit -> /1000000. Check if any corrupted are 2022
sqlite3 database.db "SELECT count(*) FROM fdbk_g1 WHERE length(ts)=15 AND strftime('%Y', ts/1000000, 'unixepoch')='2022';"
# g2 corrupted 12-digit -> /1000
sqlite3 database.db "SELECT count(*) FROM fdbk_g2 WHERE length(ts)=12 AND strftime('%Y', ts/1000, 'unixepoch')='2022';"

task: How many office product reviews were posted in calendar year 2022 for products whose catalog list price is strictly between $20 and $200 (exclude products priced exactly at those boundaries)?

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
SELECT
(SELECT count(*) FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products' AND i.prc>20 AND i.prc<200
  AND length(f.ts)=13 AND strftime('%Y', f.ts/1000, 'unixepoch')='2022') AS g1,
(SELECT count(*) FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products' AND i.prc_usd>20 AND i.prc_usd<200
  AND length(f.ts)=10 AND strftime('%Y', f.ts, 'unixepoch')='2022') AS g2,
(SELECT count(*) FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products' AND i.prc>20 AND i.prc<200
  AND f.ts LIKE '2022%') AS g3;
"

task: What is the median price of electronics products (in dollars)? Only consider products with a non-zero price. Round to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command:
python3 -c "
import sqlite3
c=sqlite3.connect('database.db')
prices=[]
for r in c.execute(\"SELECT prc FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='All Electronics' AND prc IS NOT NULL AND prc>0\"): prices.append(r[0])
for r in c.execute(\"SELECT prc_usd FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='All Electronics' AND prc_usd IS NOT NULL AND prc_usd>0\"): prices.append(r[0])
for r in c.execute(\"SELECT prc FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='All Electronics' AND prc IS NOT NULL AND prc>0\"): prices.append(r[0])
prices.sort()
import statistics
print(len(prices), round(statistics.median(prices),2))
"

task: Among electronics reviews with a review body longer than 500 characters, considering only products whose list price is at most $40, what percentage are 1-star? Round to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
SELECT
 SUM(CASE WHEN rtg=1.0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS one_star,
 COUNT(*) AS total
FROM (
 SELECT f.rtg FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
   WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND LENGTH(f.body)>500 AND i.prc<=40
 UNION ALL
 SELECT f.rtg FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
   WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND LENGTH(f.body)>500 AND i.prc_usd<=40
 UNION ALL
 SELECT f.rtg FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
   WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND LENGTH(f.body)>500 AND i.prc<=40
);"

task: Among musical instrument brands with at least 20 products (using the attributes data), which brand has the highest average review rating? Report the brand name.

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
SELECT a.attr_val AS brand, AVG(f.rtg) AS avg_rating, COUNT(DISTINCT a.ref_id) AS prod_ct, COUNT(f.rtg) AS rev_ct
FROM attrs_g3 a
JOIN fdbk_g3 f ON f.ref_id = a.ref_id
WHERE a.attr_key='Brand'
GROUP BY a.attr_val
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT a.ref_id) >= 20
ORDER BY avg_rating DESC
LIMIT 15;"

task: Among musical instrument brands with at least 20 products (using the attributes data), which brand has the highest average review rating? Report the brand name.

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH bc AS (
  SELECT attr_val AS brand, COUNT(DISTINCT ref_id) AS prod_ct
  FROM attrs_g3 WHERE attr_key='Brand' GROUP BY attr_val HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT ref_id) >= 20
)
SELECT bc.brand, AVG(f.rtg) AS avg_rating, bc.prod_ct, COUNT(f.rtg) rev
FROM bc
JOIN attrs_g3 a ON a.attr_val=bc.brand AND a.attr_key='Brand'
JOIN fdbk_g3 f ON f.ref_id=a.ref_id
GROUP BY bc.brand
ORDER BY avg_rating DESC LIMIT 5;"

task: For how many office products does the stored average rating differ from the actual mean of its review scores by more than 1 full star?

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH combined AS (
  SELECT i.ref_id, i.avg_rtg, AVG(f.rtg) AS actual FROM items_g1 i JOIN fdbk_g1 f ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products' GROUP BY i.ref_id
  UNION ALL
  SELECT i.ref_id, i.avg_rtg, AVG(f.rtg) FROM items_g2 i JOIN fdbk_g2 f ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products' GROUP BY i.ref_id
  UNION ALL
  SELECT i.ref_id, i.avg_rtg, AVG(f.rtg) FROM items_g3 i JOIN fdbk_g3 f ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products' GROUP BY i.ref_id
)
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM combined WHERE ABS(avg_rtg - actual) > 1.0;
"
