So I will assume this was made by the same crafter & from the same batch as the last. Again, the paper has been pulled too tightly & ripped through the backing. It has also been cut through the first 30% of the pages at one edge. There are paint pen marking all over the journal inside & out. This one has a cut down the front where maybe they had begun to make a scoring cut in the leather & realized it was going to be too short & then just put the design over it. They did a better job of getting a deeper embossing on this one tho. The finish is worse however. This one has a weird finish almost like pumice stones where it looks kind of crackled & porous Idk if it was done on purpose as a design or not. I wish this crafter would learn to not use paint pens to mark up leather bc it ruins the product. If you try to remove the paint pen you ruin the leather. I am double bummed for having given them a second chance.
Old review:
This could have been a beautiful little hotter notebook. Unfortunately it is not. The ppl that made it decided to use metallic paint pens & permanent markers to mark all their sewing lines ruining the aesthetic. There is not a spot on either side without a blemish. I had resolved myself that if only the inside was ugly I could live with that. Lol. Bc there’s a lot of that irl imo, but it turned out that both the inside & outside had imperfections behind what I was prepared to pay for & live with. As a disabled person I can generally live with a lot lol, but I spend my little bit of money on writing tools & I like them to be nice. This could have been a thing of true beauty as it reminds me of several Levenger notebooks I own. Sadly, while the idea was great the execution is lacking. I hope they will do it right next time bc it’s the perfect size to throw into a purse or backpack for a short trip or even a longer jaunt if you bring paper refills. The paper quality was not what I was hoping for either & as it arrived torn & already pulling away from its base I would not have used it anyways. As it is it is a hard no for me in its current state. I hope that either the seller or amazon will do right me. Someone should definitely be doing quality inspection checks bc this would not have passed even a rudimentary one. The metallic silver paint pen was a glaring no staring me right in the face the second I opened it up. The black permanent marker while it did match the journal better was still extremely obvious & still extremely annoying to see. It’s a well thought out design in my estimation just an extremely flawed execution. I hope the seller tweaks their issues & offers it in other colors bc I would totally try again.|1643160661334|0|1 2|B000KJRRBQ|B082XWH5V6|AGGZ357AO26RQZVRLGU4D4N52DZQ|4.0|Sturdy key and mail rack|Other than a slight design flaw that really warranted an exchange (the bottom mail "shelf" is welded to the frame at a little bit of an angle, causing the key hooks to also have a slight angle as well), this rack works great. I didn't feel like going through the effort of exchanging it, so I kept the one I received. It fits a lot of stuff without being too bulky on the wall (hubby said nothing wooden - that looks too "granny"!) Note that it does not come with metal plaster anchors, just screws, so you may need to make a trip to your local hardware store for some additional supplies if you have plaster or drywall. task: How many electronics products priced above $50 have at least 5 reviews and an average review rating below 3.0?

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (
  SELECT i.ref_id
  FROM items_g1 i
  JOIN fdbk_g1 f ON f.ref_id = i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat = 'All Electronics' AND i.prc > 50
  GROUP BY i.ref_id
  HAVING COUNT(f.id) >= 5 AND AVG(f.rtg) < 3.0
);
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (
  SELECT i.ref_id
  FROM items_g2 i
  JOIN fdbk_g2 f ON f.ref_id = i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat = 'All Electronics' AND i.prc_usd > 50
  GROUP BY i.ref_id
  HAVING COUNT(f.id) >= 5 AND AVG(f.rtg) < 3.0
);
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (
  SELECT i.ref_id
  FROM items_g3 i
  JOIN fdbk_g3 f ON f.ref_id = i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat = 'All Electronics' AND i.prc > 50
  GROUP BY i.ref_id
  HAVING COUNT(f.id) >= 5 AND AVG(f.rtg) < 3.0
);
"

task: How many electronics products priced above $50 have at least 5 reviews and an average review rating below 3.0?

  SELECT i.ref_id FROM items_g1 i JOIN fdbk_g1 f ON f.ref_id = i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat = 'All Electronics' AND i.prc > 50
  GROUP BY i.ref_id HAVING COUNT(f.id) >= 5 AND AVG(f.rtg) < 3.0
  UNION ALL
  SELECT i.ref_id FROM items_g2 i JOIN fdbk_g2 f ON f.ref_id = i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat = 'All Electronics' AND i.prc_usd > 50
  GROUP BY i.ref_id HAVING COUNT(f.id) >= 5 AND AVG(f.rtg) < 3.0
  UNION ALL
  SELECT i.ref_id FROM items_g3 i JOIN fdbk_g3 f ON f.ref_id = i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat = 'All Electronics' AND i.prc > 50
  GROUP BY i.ref_id HAVING COUNT(f.id) >= 5 AND AVG(f.rtg) < 3.0
);"

task: In which year-month were the most musical instrument reviews posted? Report as YYYY-MM (e.g. '2020-01').

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH mi AS (
  SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', datetime(f.ts/1000, 'unixepoch')) AS ym
  FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', datetime(f.ts, 'unixepoch')) AS ym
  FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT substr(f.ts,1,7) AS ym
  FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'
)
SELECT ym, COUNT(*) c FROM mi GROUP BY ym ORDER BY c DESC LIMIT 10;"

task: For musical instruments, how much higher (in star rating) is the average review rating for products with a listed price compared to products without a price? Round to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command: sqlite3 database.db "SELECT prc, avg_rtg FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments' LIMIT 20; SELECT '---'; SELECT prc, prc_usd, avg_rtg FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments' LIMIT 20; SELECT '---'; SELECT prc, avg_rtg FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments' LIMIT 20;"

task: For musical instruments, how much higher (in star rating) is the average review rating for products with a listed price compared to products without a price? Round to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
SELECT 
  AVG(CASE WHEN prc IS NOT NULL THEN avg_rtg END) AS with_price,
  AVG(CASE WHEN prc IS NULL THEN avg_rtg END) AS without_price,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN prc IS NOT NULL THEN 1 END) AS n_with,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN prc IS NULL THEN 1 END) AS n_without
FROM (
  SELECT prc, avg_rtg FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT prc_usd, avg_rtg FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT prc, avg_rtg FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments'
);"

task: How many office products have reviews spanning 5 or more distinct calendar years? Exclude reviews with corrupted timestamps (dates in 2031 or later).

task: How many office products have reviews spanning 5 or more distinct calendar years? Exclude reviews with corrupted timestamps (dates in 2031 or later).

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (
  SELECT f.ref_id, COUNT(DISTINCT CAST(strftime('%Y', datetime(f.ts/1000, 'unixepoch')) AS INT)) as yrs
  FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products'
    AND CAST(strftime('%Y', datetime(f.ts/1000, 'unixepoch')) AS INT) < 2031
  GROUP BY f.ref_id HAVING yrs >= 5
);
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (
  SELECT f.ref_id, COUNT(DISTINCT CAST(strftime('%Y', datetime(f.ts, 'unixepoch')) AS INT)) as yrs
  FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products'
    AND CAST(strftime('%Y', datetime(f.ts, 'unixepoch')) AS INT) < 2031
  GROUP BY f.ref_id HAVING yrs >= 5
);
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (
  SELECT f.ref_id, COUNT(DISTINCT substr(f.ts,1,4)) as yrs
  FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products'
    AND CAST(substr(f.ts,1,4) AS INT) < 2031
  GROUP BY f.ref_id HAVING yrs >= 5
);
"

task: What fraction of musical instrument reviews from repeat purchasers (users who have left 3 or more reviews in this category) are 5-star? Express as a percentage rounded to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command: sqlite3 database.db "WITH mi AS (SELECT f.uid, f.rtg FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'), repeat_users AS (SELECT uid FROM mi GROUP BY uid HAVING COUNT(*)>=3) SELECT COUNT(*) total, SUM(CASE WHEN rtg=5.0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) five_star, ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN rtg=5.0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/COUNT(*), 2) pct FROM mi WHERE uid IN (SELECT uid FROM repeat_users);"

task: How many office product reviews have timestamps that correspond to dates in the year 2031 or later?

arguments.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import sqlite3
from datetime import datetime,timezone
con=sqlite3.connect('database.db')
cur=con.cursor()
count=0

# g1: epoch ms, 13-digit normal, 15-digit /1000 to get ms
for (ts,) in cur.execute("SELECT f.ts FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products' AND f.ts IS NOT NULL"):
    if ts is None: continue
    l=len(str(ts))
    if l==15: ms=ts/1000.0
    else: ms=ts
    y=datetime.fromtimestamp(ms/1000,timezone.utc).year
    if y>=2031: count+=1

# g2: epoch sec, 10-digit normal, 12-digit /1000 to get sec
for (ts,) in cur.execute("SELECT f.ts FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products' AND f.ts IS NOT NULL"):
    if ts is None: continue
    l=len(str(ts))
    if l==12: sec=ts/1000.0
    else: sec=ts
    y=datetime.fromtimestamp(sec,timezone.utc).year
    if y>=2031: count+=1

# g3: date string YYYY-MM-DD
for (ts,) in cur.execute("SELECT f.ts FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products' AND f.ts IS NOT NULL AND f.ts!=''"):
    y=int(ts[:4])
    if y>=2031: count+=1

print("Total office product reviews with year>=2031:", count)
EOF

task: How many office product reviews have timestamps that correspond to dates in the year 2031 or later?

arguments.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import sqlite3
from datetime import datetime,timezone
con=sqlite3.connect('database.db')
cur=con.cursor()

# corrected years for office products, show top years
def yr_g1(ts):
    ms = ts/1000.0 if len(str(ts))==15 else ts
    return datetime.fromtimestamp(ms/1000,timezone.utc).year
def yr_g2(ts):
    s = ts/1000.0 if len(str(ts))==12 else ts
    return datetime.fromtimestamp(s,timezone.utc).year

for name,q,f in [
 ('g1',"SELECT f.ts FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products' AND f.ts IS NOT NULL",yr_g1),
 ('g2',"SELECT f.ts FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products' AND f.ts IS NOT NULL",yr_g2),
]:
    ys=[f(ts) for (ts,) in cur.execute(q)]
    print(name,'corrected max year:',max(ys),'count>=2031:',sum(1 for y in ys if y>=2031))
# g3
g3=[int(ts[:4]) for (ts,) in cur.execute("SELECT f.ts FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products' AND f.ts IS NOT NULL AND f.ts!=''")]
print('g3 max year:',max(g3),'count>=2031:',sum(1 for y in g3 if y>=2031))
EOF

task: What is the median price of electronics products (in dollars)? Only consider products with a non-zero price. Round to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command:
python3 -c "
import sqlite3, statistics
c=sqlite3.connect('database.db')
prices=[]
# g1: prc in dollars
for (p,) in c.execute(\"SELECT prc FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='All Electronics' AND prc IS NOT NULL AND prc>0\"):
    prices.append(p)
# g2: prefer prc_usd, else prc/100
for pu,p in c.execute(\"SELECT prc_usd, prc FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='All Electronics'\"):
    v = pu if pu is not None else (p/100.0 if p is not None else None)
    if v is not None and v>0: prices.append(v)
# g3: prc in dollars
for (p,) in c.execute(\"SELECT prc FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='All Electronics' AND prc IS NOT NULL AND prc>0\"):
    prices.append(p)
print(len(prices), round(statistics.median(prices),2))
"

echo "=== combined weighted average ==="
sqlite3 database.db "
SELECT (
 (SELECT SUM(prc) FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='All Electronics' AND prc IS NOT NULL) +

 (SELECT SUM(prc) FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='All Electronics' AND prc IS NOT NULL)
) / (
 (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='All Electronics' AND prc IS NOT NULL) +
 (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='All Electronics' AND prc_usd IS NOT NULL) +
 (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='All Electronics' AND prc IS NOT NULL)
);"

task: What is the average listed price of electronics products, expressed in dollars?

 (SELECT SUM(prc) FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='All Electronics' AND prc IS NOT NULL)
) / (
 (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='All Electronics' AND prc IS NOT NULL) +
 (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='All Electronics' AND (prc_usd IS NOT NULL OR prc IS NOT NULL)) +
 (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='All Electronics' AND prc IS NOT NULL)
);"

task: Among electronics reviews with a review body longer than 500 characters, considering only products whose list price is at most $40, what percentage are 1-star? Round to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH combined AS (
  SELECT f.rtg AS rtg, length(f.body) AS bl, i.prc AS price
  FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND i.prc IS NOT NULL
  UNION ALL
  SELECT f.rtg, length(f.body), i.prc_usd
  FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND i.prc_usd IS NOT NULL
  UNION ALL
  SELECT f.rtg, length(f.body), i.prc
  FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND i.prc IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT count(*), sum(rtg=1.0), round(100.0*sum(rtg=1.0)/count(*),2)
FROM combined WHERE bl>500 AND price<=40;
"

task: Among electronics products in the 'Computers & Accessories' top-level category, what percentage are priced above the category-wide average? Round to 2 decimal places. Only consider products with a non-zero price.

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH ca AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT i.ref_id, i.prc
  FROM items_g2 i
  JOIN taxn_g2 t ON t.ref_id = i.ref_id AND t.cat_lvl=1 AND t.cat_nm='Computers & Accessories'
  WHERE i.prc IS NOT NULL AND i.prc <> 0
),
avg AS (SELECT AVG(prc) a FROM ca)
SELECT COUNT(*) total,
       SUM(CASE WHEN prc > (SELECT a FROM avg) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) above,
       ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN prc > (SELECT a FROM avg) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/COUNT(*),2) pct
FROM ca;"

task: For verified electronics reviews, how much longer on average (in characters) are 1-star review bodies compared to 5-star review bodies? Round to 1 decimal place. Only count reviews with non-empty body text.

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
SELECT rtg, COUNT(*), ROUND(AVG(LENGTH(body)),4)
FROM (
  SELECT f.rtg, f.body FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
    WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND f.vrf='1' AND f.body IS NOT NULL AND TRIM(f.body)<>''
  UNION ALL
  SELECT f.rtg, f.body FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
    WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND f.vrf='true' AND f.body IS NOT NULL AND TRIM(f.body)<>''
  UNION ALL
  SELECT f.rtg, f.body FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
    WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND f.vrf='1' AND f.body IS NOT NULL AND TRIM(f.body)<>''
)
WHERE rtg IN (1.0,5.0)
GROUP BY rtg;
"

task: For how many office products does the stored average rating differ from the actual mean of its review scores by more than 1 full star?

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH office_items AS (
  SELECT ref_id, avg_rtg, 'g1' AS grp FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='Office Products'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT ref_id, avg_rtg, 'g2' FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='Office Products'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT ref_id, avg_rtg, 'g3' FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='Office Products'
),
review_avgs AS (
  SELECT ref_id, AVG(rtg) AS actual FROM fdbk_g1 GROUP BY ref_id
  UNION ALL
  SELECT ref_id, AVG(rtg) FROM fdbk_g2 GROUP BY ref_id
  UNION ALL
  SELECT ref_id, AVG(rtg) FROM fdbk_g3 GROUP BY ref_id
)
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM office_items o
JOIN review_avgs r ON o.ref_id=r.ref_id
WHERE ABS(o.avg_rtg - r.actual) > 1.0;
"

task: How many office products have at least one review in the database but are NOT represented in the attributes data at all?

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH office_items AS (
  SELECT ref_id FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='Office Products'
  UNION SELECT ref_id FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='Office Products'
  UNION SELECT ref_id FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='Office Products'
),
with_reviews AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT ref_id FROM fdbk_g1
  UNION SELECT DISTINCT ref_id FROM fdbk_g2
  UNION SELECT DISTINCT ref_id FROM fdbk_g3
),
in_attrs AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT ref_id FROM attrs_g1
  UNION SELECT DISTINCT ref_id FROM attrs_g3
)
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM office_items o
WHERE o.ref_id IN (SELECT ref_id FROM with_reviews)
  AND o.ref_id NOT IN (SELECT ref_id FROM in_attrs);
"

task: How many electronics products priced above $50 have at least 5 reviews and an average review rating below 3.0?

arguments.command: sqlite3 database.db "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='All Electronics' AND prc IS NOT NULL; SELECT COUNT(*) FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='All Electronics' AND prc_usd IS NOT NULL; SELECT prc, prc_usd FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='All Electronics' AND prc IS NOT NULL LIMIT 5;"

task: How many electronics products priced above $50 have at least 5 reviews and an average review rating below 3.0?

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH g1 AS (
  SELECT i.ref_id, i.prc AS price, AVG(f.rtg) AS avgr, COUNT(f.rtg) AS cnt
  FROM items_g1 i JOIN fdbk_g1 f ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics'
  GROUP BY i.ref_id
),
g2 AS (
  SELECT i.ref_id, i.prc_usd AS price, AVG(f.rtg) AS avgr, COUNT(f.rtg) AS cnt
  FROM items_g2 i JOIN fdbk_g2 f ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics'
  GROUP BY i.ref_id
),
g3 AS (
  SELECT i.ref_id, i.prc AS price, AVG(f.rtg) AS avgr, COUNT(f.rtg) AS cnt
  FROM items_g3 i JOIN fdbk_g3 f ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics'
  GROUP BY i.ref_id
)
SELECT 'g1', COUNT(*) FROM g1 WHERE price>50 AND cnt>=5 AND avgr<3.0
UNION ALL SELECT 'g2', COUNT(*) FROM g2 WHERE price>50 AND cnt>=5 AND avgr<3.0
UNION ALL SELECT 'g3', COUNT(*) FROM g3 WHERE price>50 AND cnt>=5 AND avgr<3.0;
"

task: In which year-month were the most musical instrument reviews posted? Report as YYYY-MM (e.g. '2020-01').

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH mi AS (
  SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', datetime(f.ts/1000, 'unixepoch')) ym FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', datetime(f.ts, 'unixepoch')) ym FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT substr(f.ts,1,7) ym FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'
)
SELECT ym, COUNT(*) c FROM mi WHERE ym IS NOT NULL GROUP BY ym ORDER BY c DESC LIMIT 5;"

arguments.command: sqlite3 database.db "SELECT prc, avg_rtg FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments' LIMIT 10; SELECT '---'; SELECT prc, prc_usd, avg_rtg FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments' LIMIT 10; SELECT '---'; SELECT prc, avg_rtg FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments' LIMIT 10;"

task: For musical instruments, how much higher (in star rating) is the average review rating for products with a listed price compared to products without a price? Round to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
SELECT 
  AVG(CASE WHEN prc IS NOT NULL THEN avg_rtg END) AS with_price,
  AVG(CASE WHEN prc IS NULL THEN avg_rtg END) AS without_price
FROM (
  SELECT prc, avg_rtg FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT prc, avg_rtg FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT prc, avg_rtg FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments'
);"

task: For musical instruments, how much higher (in star rating) is the average review rating for products with a listed price compared to products without a price? Round to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
SELECT ROUND(
  (SELECT AVG(avg_rtg) FROM (
     SELECT avg_rtg FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments' AND prc IS NOT NULL
     UNION ALL SELECT avg_rtg FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments' AND prc IS NOT NULL
     UNION ALL SELECT avg_rtg FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments' AND prc IS NOT NULL
   )) -
  (SELECT AVG(avg_rtg) FROM (
     SELECT avg_rtg FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments' AND prc IS NULL
     UNION ALL SELECT avg_rtg FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments' AND prc IS NULL
     UNION ALL SELECT avg_rtg FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments' AND prc IS NULL
   ))
, 2);"

task: Across both product categories that store attribute data, how many total products are listed as discontinued by the manufacturer and have a catalog average star rating strictly below 3.0?

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (
  SELECT i.ref_id FROM items_g1 i JOIN attrs_g1 a ON i.ref_id=a.ref_id
  WHERE a.attr_key LIKE 'Is Discontinued%' AND (a.attr_val='Yes' OR a.attr_val='True')
  AND i.avg_rtg < 3.0
  UNION
  SELECT i.ref_id FROM items_g3 i JOIN attrs_g3 a ON i.ref_id=a.ref_id
  WHERE a.attr_key LIKE 'Is Discontinued%' AND (a.attr_val='Yes' OR a.attr_val='True')
  AND i.avg_rtg < 3.0
);"

task: Across both product categories that store attribute data, how many total products are listed as discontinued by the manufacturer and have a catalog average star rating strictly below 3.0?

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
SELECT 'g1', COUNT(DISTINCT i.ref_id) FROM items_g1 i JOIN attrs_g1 a ON i.ref_id=a.ref_id
  WHERE a.attr_key LIKE 'Is Discontinued%' AND (a.attr_val='Yes' OR a.attr_val='True')
  AND i.avg_rtg < 3.0;
SELECT 'g3', COUNT(DISTINCT i.ref_id) FROM items_g3 i JOIN attrs_g3 a ON i.ref_id=a.ref_id
  WHERE a.attr_key LIKE 'Is Discontinued%' AND (a.attr_val='Yes' OR a.attr_val='True')
  AND i.avg_rtg < 3.0;"

task: How many office products have reviews spanning 5 or more distinct calendar years? Exclude reviews with corrupted timestamps (dates in 2031 or later).

task: How many office products have reviews spanning 5 or more distinct calendar years? Exclude reviews with corrupted timestamps (dates in 2031 or later).

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH g1 AS (
  SELECT f.ref_id, CAST(strftime('%Y', f.ts/1000, 'unixepoch') AS INTEGER) AS yr
  FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products'
),
g2 AS (
  SELECT f.ref_id, CAST(strftime('%Y', f.ts, 'unixepoch') AS INTEGER) AS yr
  FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products'
),
g3 AS (
  SELECT f.ref_id, CAST(substr(f.ts,1,4) AS INTEGER) AS yr
  FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products'
),
allr AS (
  SELECT * FROM g1 WHERE yr<2031
  UNION ALL SELECT * FROM g2 WHERE yr<2031
  UNION ALL SELECT * FROM g3 WHERE yr<2031
)
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT ref_id, COUNT(DISTINCT yr) AS ny FROM allr GROUP BY ref_id HAVING ny>=5);
"

  SELECT f.uid, f.rtg FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT f.uid, f.rtg FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT f.uid, f.rtg FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'
), repeat_users AS (SELECT uid FROM mi GROUP BY uid HAVING COUNT(*)>=3)
SELECT COUNT(*) total, SUM(CASE WHEN rtg=5.0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) five, ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN rtg=5.0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/COUNT(*),2) pct FROM mi WHERE uid IN (SELECT uid FROM repeat_users);"

task: What percentage of office product reviewers have left reviews on products spanning 2 or more different top-level categories? Round to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH ur AS (
  SELECT f.uid AS uid, i.main_cat AS cat FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  UNION ALL
  SELECT f.uid, i.main_cat FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  UNION ALL
  SELECT f.uid, i.main_cat FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
),
office AS (SELECT DISTINCT uid FROM ur WHERE cat='Office Products'),
spans AS (
  SELECT ur.uid, COUNT(DISTINCT ur.cat) AS ncat
  FROM ur JOIN office o ON ur.uid=o.uid
  WHERE ur.cat IS NOT NULL AND TRIM(ur.cat)<>''
  GROUP BY ur.uid
)
SELECT 
  (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM office) AS total_office,
  (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM spans WHERE ncat>=2) AS multi,
  ROUND(100.0*(SELECT COUNT(*) FROM spans WHERE ncat>=2)/(SELECT COUNT(*) FROM office),2) AS pct;
"

task: What percentage of office product reviewers have left reviews on products spanning 2 or more different top-level categories? Round to 2 decimal places.

sqlite3 database.db "
WITH ur AS (
  SELECT f.uid AS uid, i.main_cat AS cat FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  UNION ALL
  SELECT f.uid, i.main_cat FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  UNION ALL
  SELECT f.uid, i.main_cat FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
),
office AS (SELECT DISTINCT uid FROM ur WHERE cat='Office Products'),
spans AS (
  SELECT ur.uid, COUNT(DISTINCT ur.cat) AS ncat
  FROM ur JOIN office o ON ur.uid=o.uid
  WHERE ur.cat IS NOT NULL AND TRIM(ur.cat)<>''
  GROUP BY ur.uid
)
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM spans;  -- should equal office count
"

for g in 1 3; do echo "=== g$g prc stats ==="; sqlite3 database.db "SELECT MIN(prc), MAX(prc), AVG(prc), COUNT(prc) FROM items_g$g WHERE prc IS NOT NULL;"; done
echo "=== g2 prc/prc_usd stats ==="; sqlite3 database.db "SELECT MIN(prc), MAX(prc), AVG(prc), MIN(prc_usd), MAX(prc_usd) FROM items_g2 WHERE prc IS NOT NULL;"
echo "=== g2 check prc = prc_usd*100 ==="; sqlite3 database.db "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM items_g2 WHERE prc IS NOT NULL AND prc_usd IS NOT NULL AND ABS(prc - prc_usd*100)>1;"

task: What is the average listed price of electronics products, expressed in dollars?

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
SELECT SUM(s), SUM(c), SUM(s)/SUM(c) FROM (
  SELECT SUM(prc) s, COUNT(*) c FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='All Electronics' AND prc IS NOT NULL
  UNION ALL
  SELECT SUM(prc_usd), COUNT(*) FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='All Electronics' AND prc_usd IS NOT NULL
  UNION ALL
  SELECT SUM(prc), COUNT(*) FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='All Electronics' AND prc IS NOT NULL
);"

task: Among electronics reviews with a review body longer than 500 characters, considering only products whose list price is at most $40, what percentage are 1-star? Round to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import sqlite3
con=sqlite3.connect("database.db")
cur=con.cursor()
total=0; ones=0
# g1: prc dollars
q1="""SELECT f.rtg FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND i.prc IS NOT NULL AND i.prc<>'' AND i.prc<=40
AND f.body IS NOT NULL AND LENGTH(f.body)>500"""
# g2: prc_usd dollars
q2="""SELECT f.rtg FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND i.prc_usd IS NOT NULL AND i.prc_usd<=40
AND f.body IS NOT NULL AND LENGTH(f.body)>500"""
# g3: prc dollars
q3="""SELECT f.rtg FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND i.prc IS NOT NULL AND i.prc<>'' AND i.prc<=40
AND f.body IS NOT NULL AND LENGTH(f.body)>500"""
for q in (q1,q2,q3):
    rows=cur.execute(q).fetchall()
    total+=len(rows)
    ones+=sum(1 for r in rows if r[0]==1.0)
    print(len(rows), sum(1 for r in rows if r[0]==1.0))
print("total",total,"ones",ones,"pct",round(ones/total*100,2))
EOF

task: For verified electronics reviews, how much longer on average (in characters) are 1-star review bodies compared to 5-star review bodies? Round to 1 decimal place. Only count reviews with non-empty body text.

arguments.command:
python3 -c "
import sqlite3
con=sqlite3.connect('database.db')
c=con.cursor()
# vrf conditions per group
vrf={'1':'vrf=1','2':\"vrf='true'\",'3':'vrf=1'}
tot1=tot1c=tot5=tot5c=0
for g in ['1','2','3']:
  for star in [1,5]:
    q=f'''SELECT SUM(LENGTH(f.body)), COUNT(*) FROM fdbk_g{g} f JOIN items_g{g} i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
          WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND f.rtg={star}.0 AND {vrf[g]}
          AND f.body IS NOT NULL AND TRIM(f.body)<>'' '''
    s,ct=c.execute(q).fetchone()
    s=s or 0; ct=ct or 0
    if star==1: tot1+=s; tot1c+=ct
    else: tot5+=s; tot5c+=ct
a1=tot1/tot1c; a5=tot5/tot5c
print('1star avg',a1,tot1c)
print('5star avg',a5,tot5c)
print('diff',round(a1-a5,1))
"

"

task: How many electronics product reviews have timestamps that correspond to dates in the year 2031 or later?

arguments.command:
python3 -c "
import sqlite3
c=sqlite3.connect('database.db')
# g1 ms: 2031-01-01 in ms = 1924905600000
g1=c.execute('''SELECT count(*) FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND f.ts >= 1924905600000''').fetchone()[0]
# g2 seconds: 2031 = 1924905600
g2=c.execute('''SELECT count(*) FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND f.ts >= 1924905600''').fetchone()[0]
# g3 string date
g3=c.execute('''SELECT count(*) FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND f.ts >= '2031-01-01' ''').fetchone()[0]
print('g1',g1,'g2',g2,'g3',g3,'total',g1+g2+g3)
"

observation.content: g1 51 g2 6 g3 0 total 57

task: How many electronics product reviews have timestamps that correspond to dates in the year 2031 or later?

arguments.command:
python3 -c "
import sqlite3
c=sqlite3.connect('database.db')
rows=c.execute('SELECT ts FROM fdbk_g2 WHERE ts>2000000000').fetchall()
print('g2 anomaly count', len(rows))
for r in rows[:15]:
    print(r[0], 1970+r[0]/31557600)
"

task: How many electronics product reviews have timestamps that correspond to dates in the year 2031 or later?

arguments.command:
python3 -c "
import sqlite3
c=sqlite3.connect('database.db')
g2=c.execute('''SELECT count(*) FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND f.ts >= 1924905600''').fetchone()[0]
print('g2 electronics 2031+:', g2)
# g3 check any string >= 2031
g3any=c.execute('''SELECT count(*) FROM fdbk_g3 WHERE ts >= '2031' ''').fetchone()[0]
print('g3 any ts>=2031:', g3any)
# also check g1 non-anomalous just in case
g1n=c.execute('''SELECT count(*) FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND f.ts >= 1924905600000 AND f.ts < 2000000000000''').fetchone()[0]
print('g1 non-anomaly in 2031-2033 range:', g1n)
"

task: How many office products have a listing date before 2015?

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM items_g1 i JOIN attrs_g1 a ON i.ref_id=a.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products' AND a.attr_key='Date First Available' AND CAST(substr(a.attr_val, -4) AS INTEGER) < 2015;"
sqlite3 database.db "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM items_g3 i JOIN attrs_g3 a ON i.ref_id=a.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products' AND a.attr_key='Date First Available' AND CAST(substr(a.attr_val, -4) AS INTEGER) < 2015;"

task: How many electronics products priced above $50 have at least 5 reviews and an average review rating below 3.0?

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH elec AS (
  SELECT ref_id, prc AS price FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='All Electronics' AND prc > 50
  UNION ALL
  SELECT ref_id, prc_usd AS price FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='All Electronics' AND prc_usd > 50
  UNION ALL
  SELECT ref_id, prc AS price FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='All Electronics' AND prc > 50
),
stats AS (
  SELECT ref_id, AVG(rtg) AS ar, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM fdbk_g1 GROUP BY ref_id
  UNION ALL
  SELECT ref_id, AVG(rtg) AS ar, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM fdbk_g2 GROUP BY ref_id
  UNION ALL
  SELECT ref_id, AVG(rtg) AS ar, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM fdbk_g3 GROUP BY ref_id
)
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM elec e JOIN stats s ON e.ref_id=s.ref_id WHERE s.cnt>=5 AND s.ar<3.0;
"

  UNION ALL
  SELECT ref_id, prc AS price, 'g3' FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='All Electronics' AND prc > 50
)
SELECT src, COUNT(*) FROM elec GROUP BY src;
"

task: In which year-month were the most musical instrument reviews posted? Report as YYYY-MM (e.g. '2020-01').

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH mi AS (
  SELECT ts*1.0/1000 AS ep FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT ts*1.0 FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT strftime('%s', ts)*1.0 FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments' AND ts IS NOT NULL AND ts != ''
)
SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', ep, 'unixepoch') AS ym, COUNT(*) c FROM mi GROUP BY ym ORDER BY c DESC LIMIT 10;"

arguments.command: sqlite3 database.db "SELECT prc, avg_rtg FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments' LIMIT 20; SELECT '---'; SELECT prc, prc_usd, avg_rtg FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments' LIMIT 20; SELECT '---'; SELECT prc, avg_rtg FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments' LIMIT 20;"

task: For musical instruments, how much higher (in star rating) is the average review rating for products with a listed price compared to products without a price? Round to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH combined AS (
  SELECT prc, avg_rtg FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT prc_usd AS prc, avg_rtg FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT prc, avg_rtg FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments'
)
SELECT
  AVG(CASE WHEN prc IS NOT NULL THEN avg_rtg END) AS avg_with,
  AVG(CASE WHEN prc IS NULL THEN avg_rtg END) AS avg_without,
  ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN prc IS NOT NULL THEN avg_rtg END) - AVG(CASE WHEN prc IS NULL THEN avg_rtg END), 4) AS diff
FROM combined WHERE avg_rtg IS NOT NULL;
"

task: How many office products have reviews spanning 5 or more distinct calendar years? Exclude reviews with corrupted timestamps (dates in 2031 or later).

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH office_items AS (
  SELECT ref_id FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='Office Products'
  UNION SELECT ref_id FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='Office Products'
  UNION SELECT ref_id FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='Office Products'
),
reviews AS (
  SELECT ref_id, CAST(strftime('%Y', ts/1000, 'unixepoch') AS INT) AS yr FROM fdbk_g1
  UNION ALL
  SELECT ref_id, CAST(strftime('%Y', ts, 'unixepoch') AS INT) AS yr FROM fdbk_g2
  UNION ALL
  SELECT ref_id, CAST(substr(ts,1,4) AS INT) AS yr FROM fdbk_g3
)
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (
  SELECT r.ref_id FROM reviews r JOIN office_items o ON r.ref_id=o.ref_id
  WHERE r.yr IS NOT NULL AND r.yr < 2031
  GROUP BY r.ref_id
  HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT r.yr) >= 5
);"

task: What fraction of musical instrument reviews from repeat purchasers (users who have left 3 or more reviews in this category) are 5-star? Express as a percentage rounded to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command: sqlite3 database.db "WITH mi_reviews AS (SELECT f.uid, f.rtg FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'), repeat_users AS (SELECT uid FROM mi_reviews GROUP BY uid HAVING COUNT(*)>=3) SELECT COUNT(*) AS total, SUM(CASE WHEN rtg=5.0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS five_star, ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN rtg=5.0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/COUNT(*),2) AS pct FROM mi_reviews WHERE uid IN (SELECT uid FROM repeat_users);"

task: How many office product reviews have timestamps that correspond to dates in the year 2031 or later?

arguments.command:
echo "=== g1 (ms) ==="; sqlite3 database.db "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products' AND f.ts/1000 >= strftime('%s','2031-01-01');"
echo "=== g2 (sec) ==="; sqlite3 database.db "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products' AND f.ts >= strftime('%s','2031-01-01');"
echo "=== g3 (date str) ==="; sqlite3 database.db "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products' AND f.ts >= '2031-01-01';"

task: What is the median price of electronics products (in dollars)? Only consider products with a non-zero price. Round to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command:
python3 -c "
import sqlite3
c=sqlite3.connect('database.db')
prices=[]
# g1
for (p,) in c.execute(\"SELECT prc FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='All Electronics' AND prc IS NOT NULL AND prc>0\"):
    prices.append(p)
# g3
for (p,) in c.execute(\"SELECT prc FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='All Electronics' AND prc IS NOT NULL AND prc>0\"):
    prices.append(p)
# g2
for (prc,usd) in c.execute(\"SELECT prc, prc_usd FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='All Electronics'\"):
    v=None
    if usd is not None and usd>0: v=usd
    elif prc is not None and prc>0: v=prc/100
    if v: prices.append(v)
prices.sort()
n=len(prices)
import statistics
print('n=',n)
print('median=',round(statistics.median(prices),2))
"

task: What percentage of office product reviewers have left reviews on products spanning 2 or more different top-level categories? Round to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH allrev AS (
  SELECT f.uid AS uid, i.main_cat AS cat FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  UNION ALL
  SELECT f.uid, i.main_cat FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  UNION ALL
  SELECT f.uid, i.main_cat FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
),
office_users AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT uid FROM allrev WHERE cat='Office Products'
),
user_cats AS (
  SELECT uid, COUNT(DISTINCT cat) AS ncat FROM allrev
  WHERE uid IN (SELECT uid FROM office_users)
  GROUP BY uid
)
SELECT
  ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN ncat>=2 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/COUNT(*),2)
FROM user_cats;
"

task: What percentage of office product reviewers have left reviews on products spanning 2 or more different top-level categories? Round to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH allrev AS (
  SELECT f.uid AS uid, i.main_cat AS cat FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  UNION ALL
  SELECT f.uid, i.main_cat FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  UNION ALL
  SELECT f.uid, i.main_cat FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
),
office_users AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT uid FROM allrev WHERE cat='Office Products'
),
user_cats AS (
  SELECT uid, COUNT(DISTINCT cat) AS ncat FROM allrev
  WHERE uid IN (SELECT uid FROM office_users) AND cat IS NOT NULL AND cat<>''
  GROUP BY uid
)
SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN ncat>=2 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS spanning, COUNT(*) AS total,
  ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN ncat>=2 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/COUNT(*),2)
FROM user_cats;
"

task: Among electronics reviews with a review body longer than 500 characters, considering only products whose list price is at most $40, what percentage are 1-star? Round to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH r AS (
  SELECT f.rtg FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
    WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND i.prc<=40 AND LENGTH(f.body)>500
  UNION ALL
  SELECT f.rtg FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
    WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND i.prc_usd<=40 AND LENGTH(f.body)>500
  UNION ALL
  SELECT f.rtg FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
    WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND i.prc<=40 AND LENGTH(f.body)>500
)
SELECT COUNT(*), SUM(rtg=1.0), ROUND(100.0*SUM(rtg=1.0)/COUNT(*),2) FROM r;"

task: Among electronics products in the 'Computers & Accessories' top-level category, what percentage are priced above the category-wide average? Round to 2 decimal places. Only consider products with a non-zero price.

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH cat AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT ref_id FROM taxn_g2 WHERE cat_nm='Computers & Accessories'
),
prods AS (
  SELECT i.ref_id, i.prc_usd
  FROM items_g2 i JOIN cat ON i.ref_id=cat.ref_id
  WHERE i.prc_usd IS NOT NULL AND i.prc_usd<>0
)
SELECT COUNT(*) total,
       (SELECT AVG(prc_usd) FROM prods) avgp,
       SUM(CASE WHEN prc_usd > (SELECT AVG(prc_usd) FROM prods) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) above,
       ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN prc_usd > (SELECT AVG(prc_usd) FROM prods) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/COUNT(*),2) pct
FROM prods;"

task: How many electronics product reviews have timestamps that correspond to dates in the year 2031 or later?

arguments.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import sqlite3, datetime
con=sqlite3.connect("database.db")
cur=con.cursor()

def year_from_g1(ts):
    # ms normally; 15-digit are microseconds
    if ts is None: return None
    s=str(ts)
    if len(s)==13: sec=ts/1000
    elif len(s)==15: sec=ts/1e6
    else: sec=ts/1000
    return datetime.datetime.utcfromtimestamp(sec).year

def year_from_g2(ts):
    if ts is None: return None
    s=str(ts)
    if len(s)==10: sec=ts

python3 << 'EOF'
import sqlite3, datetime
con=sqlite3.connect("database.db")
cur=con.cursor()

def yr(sec):
    try: return datetime.datetime.utcfromtimestamp(sec).year
    except: return None

# Naive: g1 treat all as ms, g2 as seconds
for cat in ["All Electronics"]:
    # g1 naive ms
    rows=cur.execute("SELECT f.ts FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat=? AND f.ts IS NOT NULL",(cat,)).fetchall()
    c1=sum(1 for (ts,) in rows if (y:=yr(ts/1000)) and y>=2031)
    # g2 naive seconds
    rows2=cur.execute("SELECT f.ts FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat=? AND f.ts IS NOT NULL",(cat,)).fetchall()
    c2=sum(1 for (ts,) in rows2 if (y:=yr(ts)) and y>=2031)
    rows3=cur.execute("SELECT f.ts FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat=? AND f.ts IS NOT NULL AND f.ts!=''",(cat,)).fetchall()
    c3=sum(1 for (ts,) in rows3 if int(ts[:4])>=2031)
    print("naive g1",c1,"g2",c2,"g3",c3,"total",c1+c2+c3)
EOF

task: How many electronics product reviews have timestamps that correspond to dates in the year 2031 or later?

arguments.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import sqlite3, datetime
con=sqlite3.connect("database.db")
cur=con.cursor()
def yr(sec):
    try: return datetime.datetime.utcfromtimestamp(sec).year
    except: return None

# Uniform assume all ms
for g,unit in [("g1",1000),("g2",1000)]:
    rows=cur.execute(f"SELECT f.ts FROM fdbk_{g} f JOIN items_{g} i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND f.ts IS NOT NULL").fetchall()
    ys=[yr(ts/1000) for (ts,) in rows]
    c=sum(1 for y in ys if y and y>=2031)
    print(g,"uniform-ms >=2031:",c, "range", min(x for x in ys if x),max(x for x in ys if x))
# Uniform assume all seconds
for g in ["g1","g2"]:
    rows=cur.execute(f"SELECT f.ts FROM fdbk_{g} f JOIN items_{g} i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND f.ts IS NOT NULL").fetchall()
    ys=[yr(ts) for (ts,) in rows]
    c=sum(1 for y in ys if y and y>=2031)
    vals=[x for x in ys if x]
    print(g,"uniform-sec >=2031:",c,"range",min(vals),max(vals))
EOF

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (
  SELECT i.ref_id FROM items_g1 i
  JOIN fdbk_g1 f ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND i.prc>50
  GROUP BY i.ref_id
  HAVING COUNT(*)>=5 AND AVG(f.rtg)<3.0
);
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (
  SELECT i.ref_id FROM items_g2 i
  JOIN fdbk_g2 f ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND i.prc_usd>50
  GROUP BY i.ref_id
  HAVING COUNT(*)>=5 AND AVG(f.rtg)<3.0
);
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (
  SELECT i.ref_id FROM items_g3 i
  JOIN fdbk_g3 f ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND i.prc>50
  GROUP BY i.ref_id
  HAVING COUNT(*)>=5 AND AVG(f.rtg)<3.0
);"

1|B08L4K4QKR|B08L4K4QKR|AFKZENTNBQ7A7V7UXW5JJI6UGRYQ|3.0|Could have been wonderful. Has execution flaws.|Update: received replacement & the second is just like the first unfortunately. So I will assume this was made by the same crafter & from the same batch as the last. Again, the paper has been pulled too tightly & ripped through the backing. It has also been cut through the first 30% of the pages at one edge. There are paint pen marking all over the journal inside & out. This one has a cut down the front where maybe they had begun to make a scoring cut in the leather & realized it was going to be too short & then just put the design over it. They did a better job of getting a deeper embossing on this one tho. The finish is worse however. This one has a weird finish almost like pumice stones where it looks kind of crackled & porous Idk if it was done on purpose as a design or not. I wish this crafter would learn to not use paint pens to mark up leather bc it ruins the product. If you try to remove the paint pen you ruin the leather. I am double bummed for having given them a second chance.
Old review:
This could have been a beautiful little hotter notebook. Unfortunately it is not. The ppl that made it decided to use metallic paint pens & permanent markers to mark all their sewing lines ruining the aesthetic. There is not a spot on either side without a blemish. I had resolved myself that if only the inside was ugly I could live with that. Lol. Bc there’s a lot of that irl imo, but it turned out that both the inside & outside had imperfections behind what I was prepared to pay for & live with. As a disabled person I can generally live with a lot lol, but I spend my little bit of money on writing tools & I like them to be nice. This could have been a thing of true beauty as it reminds me of several Levenger notebooks I own. Sadly, while the idea was great the execution is lacking. I hope they will do it right next time bc it’s the perfect size to throw into a purse or backpack for a short trip or even a longer jaunt if you bring paper refills. The paper quality was not what I was hoping for either & as it arrived torn & already pulling away from its base I would not have used it anyways. As it is it is a hard no for me in its current state. I hope that either the seller or amazon will do right me. Someone should definitely be doing quality inspection checks bc this would not have passed even a rudimentary one. The metallic silver paint pen was a glaring no staring me right in the face the second I opened it up. The black permanent marker while it did match the journal better was still extremely obvious & still extremely annoying to see. It’s a well thought out design in my estimation just an extremely flawed execution. I hope the seller tweaks their issues & offers it in other colors bc I would totally try again.|1643160661334|0|1 2|B000KJRRBQ|B082XWH5V6|AGGZ357AO26RQZVRLGU4D4N52DZQ|4.0|Sturdy key and mail rack|Other than a slight design flaw that really warranted an exchange (the bottom mail "shelf" is welded to the frame at a little bit of an angle, causing the key hooks to also have a slight angle as well), this rack works great. It fits a lot of stuff without being too bulky on the wall (hubby said nothing wooden - that looks too "granny"!) Note that it does not come with metal plaster anchors, just screws, so you may need to make a trip to your local hardware store for some additional supplies if you have plaster or drywall.   SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', ts/1000, 'unixepoch') ym FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', ts, 'unixepoch') FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT substr(ts,1,7) FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'
)
SELECT ym, COUNT(*) c FROM mi GROUP BY ym ORDER BY c DESC LIMIT 10;"

task: For musical instruments, how much higher (in star rating) is the average review rating for products with a listed price compared to products without a price? Round to 2 decimal places.
For musical instruments, how much higher (in star rating) is the average review rating for products with a listed price compared to products without a price? Round to 2 decimal places.
For musical instruments, how much higher (in star rating) is the average review rating for products with a listed price compared to products without a price? Round to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH combined AS (
  SELECT prc, avg_rtg FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments' AND avg_rtg IS NOT NULL
  UNION ALL
  SELECT prc, avg_rtg FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments' AND avg_rtg IS NOT NULL
  UNION ALL
  SELECT prc, avg_rtg FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments' AND avg_rtg IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
  AVG(CASE WHEN prc IS NOT NULL THEN avg_rtg END) as with_price,
  AVG(CASE WHEN prc IS NULL THEN avg_rtg END) as without_price,
  ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN prc IS NOT NULL THEN avg_rtg END) - AVG(CASE WHEN prc IS NULL THEN avg_rtg END), 2) as diff
FROM combined;"

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH combined AS (
  SELECT i.prc as prc, f.rtg as rtg FROM items_g1 i JOIN fdbk_g1 f ON i.ref_id=f.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments' AND f.rtg IS NOT NULL
  UNION ALL
  SELECT i.prc, f.rtg FROM items_g2 i JOIN fdbk_g2 f ON i.ref_id=f.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments' AND f.rtg IS NOT NULL
  UNION ALL
  SELECT i.prc, f.rtg FROM items_g3 i JOIN fdbk_g3 f ON i.ref_id=f.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments' AND f.rtg IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
  AVG(CASE WHEN prc IS NOT NULL THEN rtg END) as with_price,
  AVG(CASE WHEN prc IS NULL THEN rtg END) as without_price,
  ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN prc IS NOT NULL THEN rtg END) - AVG(CASE WHEN prc IS NULL THEN rtg END), 2) as diff
FROM combined;"

task: How many office products have reviews spanning 5 or more distinct calendar years? Exclude reviews with corrupted timestamps (dates in 2031 or later).

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH office_items AS (
  SELECT ref_id, 'g1' AS grp FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='Office Products'
  UNION ALL SELECT ref_id, 'g2' FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='Office Products'
  UNION ALL SELECT ref_id, 'g3' FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='Office Products'
),
years AS (
  SELECT o.ref_id, CAST(strftime('%Y', f.ts/1000, 'unixepoch') AS INTEGER) AS yr
  FROM office_items o JOIN fdbk_g1 f ON o.ref_id=f.ref_id WHERE o.grp='g1'
  UNION
  SELECT o.ref_id, CAST(strftime('%Y', f.ts, 'unixepoch') AS INTEGER)
  FROM office_items o JOIN fdbk_g2 f ON o.ref_id=f.ref_id WHERE o.grp='g2'
  UNION
  SELECT o.ref_id, CAST(substr(f.ts,1,4) AS INTEGER)
  FROM office_items o JOIN fdbk_g3 f ON o.ref_id=f.ref_id WHERE o.grp='g3'
)
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (
  SELECT ref_id FROM years WHERE yr < 2031 AND yr IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY ref_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT yr) >= 5
);
"

task: What fraction of musical instrument reviews from repeat purchasers (users who have left 3 or more reviews in this category) are 5-star? Express as a percentage rounded to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command: sqlite3 database.db "WITH mi AS (SELECT f.uid, f.rtg FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'), repeat AS (SELECT uid FROM mi GROUP BY uid HAVING COUNT(*)>=3) SELECT COUNT(*) total, SUM(CASE WHEN rtg=5 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) fives, ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN rtg=5 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/COUNT(*),2) pct FROM mi WHERE uid IN (SELECT uid FROM repeat);"

task: What is the median price of electronics products (in dollars)? Only consider products with a non-zero price. Round to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command:
python3 -c "
import sqlite3
c=sqlite3.connect('database.db')
prices=[]
for r in c.execute(\"SELECT prc FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='All Electronics' AND prc>0\"):
    prices.append(r[0])
for r in c.execute(\"SELECT prc FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='All Electronics' AND prc>0\"):
    prices.append(r[0])
for prc,usd in c.execute(\"SELECT prc, prc_usd FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='All Electronics'\"):
    v = usd if (usd is not None and usd>0) else (prc/100.0 if prc else 0)
    if v>0: prices.append(v)
import statistics
prices.sort()
print('count',len(prices))
print('median',round(statistics.median(prices),2))
"

observation.content:
count 2775
median 24.49

arguments.command:
for g in 1 2 3; do
echo "=== group $g ==="
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH elec AS (
  SELECT ref_id, prc FROM items_g$g WHERE main_cat='All Electronics'
),
rc AS (
  SELECT ref_id, COUNT(*) c FROM fdbk_g$g GROUP BY ref_id
)
SELECT 
  SUM(CASE WHEN COALESCE(rc.c,0)=0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS zero_rev,

FROM elec e LEFT JOIN rc ON e.ref_id=rc.ref_id;
"

task: Among electronics products that have zero reviews, what percentage have a price listed (non-NULL, non-zero)? Round to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH elec AS (
  SELECT ref_id, prc FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='All Electronics'
  UNION ALL SELECT ref_id, prc FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='All Electronics'
  UNION ALL SELECT ref_id, prc FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='All Electronics'
),
rc AS (
  SELECT ref_id FROM fdbk_g1 UNION ALL SELECT ref_id FROM fdbk_g2 UNION ALL SELECT ref_id FROM fdbk_g3
),
cnt AS (SELECT ref_id, COUNT(*) c FROM rc GROUP BY ref_id)
SELECT 
  SUM(CASE WHEN COALESCE(cnt.c,0)=0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS zero_rev,
  SUM(CASE WHEN COALESCE(cnt.c,0)=0 AND e.prc IS NOT NULL AND e.prc<>0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS zero_rev_priced,
  ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN COALESCE(cnt.c,0)=0 AND e.prc IS NOT NULL AND e.prc<>0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/SUM(CASE WHEN COALESCE(cnt.c,0)=0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END),2) AS pct
FROM elec e LEFT JOIN cnt ON e.ref_id=cnt.ref_id;
"

observation.content:
=== g1 sample body === Update: received replacement & the second is just like the first unfortunately. So I will assume this was made by the same crafter & from the same batch as the last. Again, the paper has been pulled too tightly & ripped through the backing. It has also been cut through the first 30% of the pages at one edge. There are paint pen marking all over the journal inside & out. This one has a cut down the front where maybe they had begun to make a scoring cut in the leather & realized it was going to be too short & then just put the design over it. They did a better job of getting a deeper embossing on this one tho. The finish is worse however. This one has a weird finish almost like pumice stones where it looks kind of crackled & porous Idk if it was done on purpose as a design or not. I wish this crafter would learn to not use paint pens to mark up leather bc it ruins the product. If you try to remove the paint pen you ruin the leather. I am double bummed for having given them a second chance.
Old review:
This could have been a beautiful little hotter notebook. Unfortunately it is not. The ppl that made it decided to use metallic paint pens & permanent markers to mark all their sewing lines ruining the aesthetic. There is not a spot on either side without a blemish. I had resolved myself that if only the inside was ugly I could live with that. Lol. Bc there’s a lot of that irl imo, but it turned out that both the inside & outside had imperfections behind what I was prepared to pay for & live with. As a disabled person I can generally live with a lot lol, but I spend my little bit of money on writing tools & I like them to be nice. This could have been a thing of true beauty as it reminds me of several Levenger notebooks I own. Sadly, while the idea was great the execution is lacking. I hope they will do it right next time bc it’s the perfect size to throw into a purse or backpack for a short trip or even a longer jaunt if you bring paper refills. The paper quality was not what I was hoping for either & as it arrived torn & already pulling away from its base I would not have used it anyways. As it is it is a hard no for me in its current state. I hope that either the seller or amazon will do right me. Someone should definitely be doing quality inspection checks bc this would not have passed even a rudimentary one. The metallic silver paint pen was a glaring no staring me right in the face the second I opened it up. The black permanent marker while it did match the journal better was still extremely obvious & still extremely annoying to see. It’s a well thought out design in my estimation just an extremely flawed execution. I hope the seller tweaks their issues & offers it in other colors bc I would totally try again. Other than a slight design flaw that really warranted an exchange (the bottom mail "shelf" is welded to the frame at a little bit of an angle, causing the key hooks to also have a slight angle as well), this rack works great. I didn't feel like going through the effort of exchanging it, so I kept the one I received. It fits a lot of stuff without being too bulky on the wall (hubby said nothing wooden - that looks too "granny"!) Note that it does not come with metal plaster anchors, just screws, so you may need to make a trip to your local hardware store for some additional supplies if you have plaster or drywall. Overall, pleased with the product compared to others available for purchase. The wheel size mimics an inline hockey goaltender wheel which effectively lowers your chair between 1/2 to 1 full inch.

Sturdy for the price, but don't expect skate worthy bearings

Whole unit comes dry from the factory so if you have a high quality lube, use it.

Good buy. I got two sets. 1 black and 1 blue. Too bad red is not available. === g2 sample body === Works great. Son uses it to hold Beats and Jaybirds. Traveled well and protected items. Great camera! i am really pleased: Just what I was looking for. . . These are the best and most reasonably priced earbuds I have ever had. I use them when I run and they consistently perform well. === g3 sample body === Xlnt quality. Does NOT work for thick music books. Xlnt for half inch thick music books. Great item This was a great purchase! I need this mic boom to film my podcast and online courses. This is perfect and is an extremely great deal. Thanks for an easy and fast transaction!

task: For verified electronics reviews, how much longer on average (in characters) are 1-star review bodies compared to 5-star review bodies? Round to 1 decimal place. Only count reviews with non-empty body text.

arguments.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import sqlite3
con=sqlite3.connect("database.db")
c=con.cursor()

def stats(g, vrf_true):
    q=f"""SELECT f.rtg, LENGTH(f.body) FROM fdbk_g{g} f
          JOIN items_g{g} i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
          WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics'
            AND f.vrf={vrf_true!r}
            AND f.body IS NOT NULL AND TRIM(f.body)<>''
            AND f.rtg IN (1.0,5.0)"""
    return c.execute(q).fetchall()

rows=[]
rows+=stats(1,1)
rows+=stats(3,1)
# g2 vrf text
q="""SELECT f.rtg, LENGTH(f.body) FROM fdbk_g2 f
     JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
     WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND f.vrf='true'
       AND f.body IS NOT NULL AND TRIM(f.body)<>'' AND f.rtg IN (1.0,5.0)"""
rows+=c.execute(q).fetchall()

one=[l for r,l in rows if r==1.0]
five=[l for r,l in rows if r==5.0]
a1=sum(one)/len(one); a5=sum(five)/len(five)
print("1-star count",len(one),"avg",a1)
print("5-star count",len(five),"avg",a5)
print("diff",round(a1-a5,1))

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
SELECT a.attr_val AS brand, COUNT(DISTINCT i.ref_id) AS n, ROUND(AVG(i.avg_rtg),4) AS avg_rating
FROM items_g3 i
JOIN attrs_g3 a ON a.ref_id=i.ref_id AND a.attr_key='Brand'
WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'
GROUP BY a.attr_val

ORDER BY avg_rating DESC

task: Among musical instrument brands with at least 20 products (using the attributes data), which brand has the highest average review rating? Report the brand name.

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH mi AS (
  SELECT ref_id FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL SELECT ref_id FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments'
),
br AS (
  SELECT ref_id, attr_val AS brand FROM attrs_g1 WHERE attr_key='Brand'
  UNION ALL SELECT ref_id, attr_val FROM attrs_g3 WHERE attr_key='Brand'
),
rv AS (
  SELECT ref_id, rtg FROM fdbk_g1 UNION ALL SELECT ref_id, rtg FROM fdbk_g3
)
SELECT br.brand, COUNT(DISTINCT mi.ref_id) n, ROUND(AVG(rv.rtg),4) ar
FROM mi JOIN br ON br.ref_id=mi.ref_id JOIN rv ON rv.ref_id=mi.ref_id
GROUP BY br.brand HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT mi.ref_id)>=20
ORDER BY ar DESC LIMIT 5;"

WITH office AS (

  UNION ALL

),
revs AS (
  SELECT ref_id, AVG(rtg) as m FROM fdbk_g1 GROUP BY ref_id
  UNION ALL
  SELECT ref_id, AVG(rtg) as m FROM fdbk_g2 GROUP BY ref_id
  UNION ALL
  SELECT ref_id, AVG(rtg) as m FROM fdbk_g3 GROUP BY ref_id
)

"

task: How many electronics products priced above $50 have at least 5 reviews and an average review rating below 3.0?

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (
  SELECT i.ref_id, AVG(f.rtg) avgr, COUNT(f.rtg) cnt
  FROM items_g1 i JOIN fdbk_g1 f ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND i.prc > 50
  GROUP BY i.ref_id
  HAVING cnt >= 5 AND avgr < 3.0
);
SELECT '---g2---';
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (
  SELECT i.ref_id, AVG(f.rtg) avgr, COUNT(f.rtg) cnt
  FROM items_g2 i JOIN fdbk_g2 f ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND i.prc_usd > 50
  GROUP BY i.ref_id
  HAVING cnt >= 5 AND avgr < 3.0
);
SELECT '---g3---';
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (
  SELECT i.ref_id, AVG(f.rtg) avgr, COUNT(f.rtg) cnt
  FROM items_g3 i JOIN fdbk_g3 f ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='All Electronics' AND i.prc > 50
  GROUP BY i.ref_id
  HAVING cnt >= 5 AND avgr < 3.0
);"

task: In which year-month were the most musical instrument reviews posted? Report as YYYY-MM (e.g. '2020-01').

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH mi AS (
  SELECT ref_id FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION SELECT ref_id FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION SELECT ref_id FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments'
),
r AS (
  SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', datetime(ts/1000,'unixepoch')) ym FROM fdbk_g1 WHERE ref_id IN (SELECT ref_id FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments')
  UNION ALL
  SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', datetime(ts,'unixepoch')) FROM fdbk_g2 WHERE ref_id IN (SELECT ref_id FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments')
  UNION ALL
  SELECT substr(ts,1,7) FROM fdbk_g3 WHERE ref_id IN (SELECT ref_id FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments')
)
SELECT ym, COUNT(*) c FROM r GROUP BY ym ORDER BY c DESC LIMIT 10;"

task: For musical instruments, how much higher (in star rating) is the average review rating for products with a listed price compared to products without a price? Round to 2 decimal places.
For musical instruments, how much higher (in star rating) is the average review rating for products with a listed price compared to products without a price? Round to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command: sqlite3 database.db "SELECT prc, avg_rtg FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments' LIMIT 20;"

task: For musical instruments, how much higher (in star rating) is the average review rating for products with a listed price compared to products without a price? Round to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH combined AS (
  SELECT prc, avg_rtg FROM items_g1 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT prc, avg_rtg FROM items_g2 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT prc, avg_rtg FROM items_g3 WHERE main_cat='Musical Instruments'
)
SELECT 
  AVG(CASE WHEN prc IS NOT NULL THEN avg_rtg END) as with_price,
  AVG(CASE WHEN prc IS NULL THEN avg_rtg END) as without_price,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN prc IS NOT NULL THEN 1 END) as with_ct,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN prc IS NULL THEN 1 END) as without_ct
FROM combined WHERE avg_rtg IS NOT NULL;"

task: Across both product categories that store attribute data, how many total products are listed as discontinued by the manufacturer and have a catalog average star rating strictly below 3.0?

arguments.command: sqlite3 database.db "SELECT DISTINCT attr_key FROM attrs_g1;" && echo "---" && sqlite3 database.db "SELECT DISTINCT attr_key FROM attrs_g3;"

task: How many office products have reviews spanning 5 or more distinct calendar years? Exclude reviews with corrupted timestamps (dates in 2031 or later).

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH office_reviews AS (
  SELECT i.ref_id, CAST(strftime('%Y', ts/1000, 'unixepoch') AS INT) yr
  FROM items_g1 i JOIN fdbk_g1 f ON i.ref_id=f.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT i.ref_id, CAST(strftime('%Y', ts, 'unixepoch') AS INT) yr
  FROM items_g2 i JOIN fdbk_g2 f ON i.ref_id=f.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT i.ref_id, CAST(substr(ts,1,4) AS INT) yr
  FROM items_g3 i JOIN fdbk_g3 f ON i.ref_id=f.ref_id
  WHERE i.main_cat='Office Products'
)
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (
  SELECT ref_id FROM office_reviews
  WHERE yr < 2031 AND yr IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY ref_id
  HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT yr) >= 5
);
"

task: What fraction of musical instrument reviews from repeat purchasers (users who have left 3 or more reviews in this category) are 5-star? Express as a percentage rounded to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH mi_reviews AS (
  SELECT uid, rtg FROM fdbk_g1 f JOIN items_g1 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT uid, rtg FROM fdbk_g2 f JOIN items_g2 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT uid, rtg FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'
),
repeat_users AS (
  SELECT uid FROM mi_reviews GROUP BY uid HAVING COUNT(*) >= 3
)
SELECT 
  SUM(CASE WHEN r.rtg=5.0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as five_star,
  COUNT(*) as total,
  ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN r.rtg=5.0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/COUNT(*), 2) as pct
FROM mi_reviews r
WHERE r.uid IN (SELECT uid FROM repeat_users);
"

task: What fraction of musical instrument reviews from repeat purchasers (users who have left 3 or more reviews in this category) are 5-star? Express as a percentage rounded to 2 decimal places.

arguments.command:
sqlite3 database.db "
WITH mi_reviews AS (
  SELECT uid, rtg FROM fdbk_g3 f JOIN items_g3 i ON f.ref_id=i.ref_id WHERE i.main_cat='Musical Instruments'
),
repeat_users AS (
  SELECT uid FROM mi_reviews GROUP BY uid HAVING COUNT(*) >= 3
)
SELECT 
  SUM(CASE WHEN r.rtg=5.0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as five_star,
  COUNT(*) as total,
  ROUND(100.0*SUM(CASE WHEN r.rtg=5.0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/COUNT(*), 2) as pct
FROM mi_reviews r
WHERE r.uid IN (SELECT uid FROM repeat_users);
"
